decisions.title: scripts/orchestrator/* function as canonical test surface + executable specification of extension surface requirements
decisions.context: POC TypeScript scripts under scripts/orchestrator/ (extract-mandates, extract-decs, extract-feedback, build-subagent-preamble, file-block-item, build-explore-brief landed at HEAD fa59884) were initially framed as orchestrator-side ergonomics wrappers replacing hand-construction variance in subagent briefs. Observed during their construction: each script's implementation surfaces a concrete gap in the extension surface — extract-feedback exposes memory-vs-substrate boundary violation (closed by FGAP-033 candidate); extract-mandates exposes mandate substrate-canon home gap; extract-decs exposes per-item macros library incompleteness (REVIEW-001); file-block-item exposes schema-aware filing UX gap that pre-existed for both Claude-Code orchestrator AND in-pi JIT agents; build-subagent-preamble exposes per-type context composition gap (DEC-0017 context-contracts territory). The scripts thereby double as both consumers of the canonical surface AND executable specifications of what the canonical surface must offer for agents to execute their work.
decisions.decision: scripts/orchestrator/*.ts have a CANONICAL DUAL ROLE per DEC-0019: (1) Claude-Code-side ergonomics wrappers around block-api / extension surface (per DEC-0014/0016 dual-surface pattern); AND (2) executable specifications of extension surface requirements that surface gaps via the act of being written. New scripts under scripts/orchestrator/ are evaluated on both axes: do they replace hand-construction variance? + do they surface gaps that become FGAPs driving extension implementation? Each gap surfaced is filed as FGAP (or referenced as existing FGAP) before script lands. Scripts are not throwaway POCs — they are persistent test surface for the extension API contract.
consequences: Every new script under scripts/orchestrator/ is reviewed for which extension primitives it consumes + which it forces into existence. The list goes in the script's header docstring as the SURFACED-GAPS section.

FGAPs surfaced by scripts are first-class — not informational notes in commit bodies. Each gets file-block-item invocation to file in framework-gaps.json with cross-reference to the script that surfaced it.

Tool-surface gap audit (analysis/2026-05-10-tool-surface-gap-audit.md) becomes partially auto-populated by the script-writing process — scripts that need primitives not yet a Pi tool = confirmed gap in the audit's scope.

Test surface property: when a Pi tool / block-api primitive / DispatchContext shape changes, the affected scripts are the canary — their breakage signals consumer-facing impact. Includes explicit script-to-consumer-need mapping kept current as scripts are added.
Dual-direction value drives priority: a gap that blocks BOTH a script AND a Pi tool consumer = higher priority than one that blocks only one direction.

Articulation of extension requirements becomes Claude-Code-readable in addition to schema-readable. A future Claude session reading scripts/orchestrator/ has a working executable map of what the extension surface offers vs needs.

Per the just-landed dual-surface pattern (feedback_orchestrator_scripts_dual_surface.md): library + Pi tool + CLI script land as a unit. DEC-0019 adds: the CLI script ALSO articulates the API contract requirement set surfaced during its writing — these are inputs to FGAP filings + tool-surface audit refresh.

decisions.title: Per-layer work-unit outputs are typed substrate blocks (cascade-target / plan-step / impl-applied / demo-result / probe-result); agents block-write items; markdown is display-only; layered composability via work_unit_id + readBlock+filter
decisions.context: Pieces of this pattern exist scattered across canon: DEC-0017 names work-unit context composition substrate; FGAP-029/030/031/032 cluster covers bidirectional traversal + per-unit-kind context contract + gather-execution-context primitive + item-level contextBlocks; FGAP-034 enumerates 6 elided block kinds (cascade-target / audit-result / arc-cross-ref / import-chain / investigation / brief-history); DEC-0019 frames scripts/orchestrator/* as test surface that surfaces gaps; REVIEW-001 names per-item macros pending. The COHERENT LAYERED PATTERN that ties these pieces — explore → cascade-target items, plan → plan-step items, implement → impl-applied items, demo → demo-result items, probe → probe-result items; each layer reads prior layer's items via readBlock+filter where work_unit_id matches; each layer's output IS substrate (not markdown intermediate) — is implicit but not yet articulated as canon. Markdown-as-load-bearing-intermediate produced the C.3 v2 explore agent prose-summary contradiction; FGAP-034 enumerated the 6 specific elided kinds; this DEC canonicalizes the broader architectural position.
decisions.decision: Every work-unit (sub-section commit, sub-phase commit, etc.) produces per-layer outputs as TYPED SUBSTRATE BLOCKS (one block_kind per layer-output type). All layer-output items carry a work_unit_id field associating them with the work unit they describe. Layer composability flow: explore agent block-writes cascade-target / audit-result / arc-cross-ref items; plan agent reads explore items via readBlock+filter+work_unit_id, block-writes plan-step items; implement agent reads cascade-target + plan-step, block-writes impl-applied items + commit-sha; demo agent block-writes demo-result items per DEC-0018 probe; adversarial-probe agent block-writes probe-result items. Markdown rendering becomes display-only via macros (one per layer-output block_kind); substrate is canonical. Agents — both in-pi (Pi tool dispatch) and Claude Code (file-block-item.ts wrapper) — block-write items rather than markdown reports. Status cascades + cross-session continuity + replay + adversarial verification all become substrate operations (readBlock+filter), not markdown-grep operations.
consequences: FGAP-034's 6 elided block kinds (cascade-target / audit-result / arc-cross-ref / import-chain / investigation / brief-history) become the initial implementation set; closing FGAP-034 closes the substrate side of DEC-0020.

Per-item macros (REVIEW-001 territory) become per-layer-output kind: render_cascade_target / render_audit_result / render_plan_step / render_impl_applied / render_demo_result / render_probe_result. Each closure feeds back into REVIEW-001.

FGAP-029/030/031/032 cluster becomes load-bearing for layered composability: gather_for_unit(work_unit_id) returns the cross-layer item set; per-unit-kind context contract declares which layer-blocks a unit composes from; bidirectional traversal walks the layer-output edges.

DEC-0017 (work-unit context composition substrate) gains concrete content: the units composed are layer-output items per work_unit_id; the contracts declare per-unit-kind block-kind dependencies; the composition primitive is gather_for_unit.

scripts/orchestrator/* composers (build-explore-brief / build-implementation-brief / future build-plan-brief / build-demo-brief / build-probe-brief) shift from markdown-section extraction to readBlock+filter; extract-markdown-section.ts becomes deprecated as DEC-0020 implementations land.

Agent dispatch discipline: every agent's task spec ends with 'block-write your output items via Pi tool / file-block-item.ts wrapper; NO markdown report file as deliverable'. Markdown rendering happens orchestrator-side via macros consuming the substrate.

TABLES-ONLY rule (Explore brief output_format) becomes redundant once substrate-block-write replaces markdown-output entirely; the rule's spirit (no LLM aggregation hallucination over tabular data) IS what DEC-0020 codifies architecturally.

Phase 5 of FGAP-026 closure (arc-tracking-blocks dogfood substrate per FGAP-028) becomes the first opportunity to dogfood DEC-0020: the arc-tracking blocks ARE per-layer-output items for the FGAP-026 closure work unit.

Cross-session continuity property: any future session can readBlock + filter on work_unit_id to reconstruct the entire arc of a work unit (explore findings → plan → impl → demos → probes), without depending on /tmp/ markdown files.

Standing tension with implementation velocity: each new orchestrator-side script needs its output kind designed + schema'd + macro'd before lands. Accepted as cost of correctness; per DEC-0019's iteration model, gaps surface during script writing and feed back to schema design.

decisions.title: Hard-throw substrate primitive cascades per-package atomically — source + absent-substrate-catch + test-fixture cascade land together per consumer package; phase-split-by-concern-type retired
decisions.context: FGAP-026 phase 1.2 Section C.3 (commit d64cb33 — pi-workflows test fixture cascade) demonstrated empirically that splitting per-consumer-package work across phases by concern-type is structurally invalid when the underlying substrate primitive is hard-throw (DEC-0015's resolveContextDir). Plan modeled cascade as: fixture-cascade per package in Phase 1.2 sections C.2/C.3/C.4; source-cascade per package in Phase 7. C.3 implementation cascaded fixtures correctly per the plan (138 sites cascaded + 55 skipped per explore classification) but 20/826 pi-workflows tests still failed because: (a) explore agent misclassified 2 test files (loop.test.ts + resume.test.ts) as no-resolver-reach when they transitively reach resolver via executeWorkflow → readBlock — 16 of the 20 failures; (b) pi-workflows production source (step-shared.ts) hardcodes .project/schemas/<name>.schema.json for block: prefix expansion in resolveSchemaPath — 3 failures that fixture cascade alone CANNOT fix; (c) pi-workflows compileAgentSpec gracefully-skipped block injection when .project/ did not exist (pre-DEC-0015 via loadConfig→null), now throws BootstrapNotFoundError under DEC-0015 strict — 1 failure that fixture cascade alone CANNOT fix. The fixture-vs-source phase split was an LLM-rationalized abstraction; reality is atomic-by-package. C.2 pi-jit-agents (commit 99436c3) lucky-passed because pi-jit-agents source had no hardcoded .project literals AND no absent-substrate-graceful-skip code paths — the principle was wrong but the consequence happened to be invisible. Retroactive C.2 audit + pre-dispatch C.4 audit required to confirm pattern hold.
decisions.decision: When a hard-throw substrate primitive lands (e.g. resolveContextDir per DEC-0015, OR any future primitive that hard-throws on absent declared state), per-consumer-package cascade is ATOMIC: source-cascade + absent-substrate-catch + test-fixture cascade land together per arc (single commit OR sequential commits within one sub-phase). Splitting these across phases by concern-type is forbidden because hard-throw is atomic across all consumers — there is no fixture-only half-state, no source-only half-state. Specifically per-consumer-package atomic gates: (1) SOURCE CASCADE — hardcoded substrate-dir literals in production source → resolver-cascaded helpers per DEC-0015 strict; (2) ABSENT-SUBSTRATE CATCH — production code paths that gracefully-degraded pre-hard-throw via null-return → catch the typed error (BootstrapNotFoundError / equivalent) + preserve the graceful-skip semantic post-hard-throw, same pattern as audit-fix cluster D-B-3/D-B-4 added at addWork + installProject tool entry points; (3) TEST FIXTURE CASCADE — tests that exercise resolver-cascading paths (direct OR transitive) → write bootstrap pointer / equivalent fixture priming. Pre-dispatch audit for any consumer-package cascade: grep production source for hardcoded substrate-dir literals (gate 1 surface); enumerate absent-substrate-handling code paths from pre-hard-throw era (gate 2 surface); enumerate per-test-file resolver-reach including transitive (gate 3 surface). The canonical sub-phase analysis (analysis/2026-05-10-fgap-026-closure-sub-phase-structure.md) Phase 7 row + Phase 1.2 cascade sections are rewritten per DEC-0021: each C.* section for a consumer package includes all 3 gates; Phase 7 is retired as a separate stage (its concerns are subsumed into C.* atomic units).
consequences: FGAP-026 phase 1.2 C.3 completion requires 3 atomic gates: cascade resolveSchemaPath in step-shared.ts (production source — gate 1) + catch BootstrapNotFoundError in compileAgentSpec (absent-substrate-catch — gate 2) + fixture cascade for misclassified loop.test.ts + resume.test.ts (fixture cascade fix — gate 3). All three land before C.3 declared complete.

Retroactive C.2 pi-jit-agents source audit: grep production source for hardcoded .project literals + enumerate absent-substrate-handling code paths. If gaps found, file as C.2-followup atomic-gate work; if confirmed clean, file an audit note in the C.2 commit chain.

Pre-dispatch C.4 pi-behavior-monitors audit: same 3-gate enumeration MUST happen before any C.4 dispatch. Cannot blindly trust the C.2 pattern held; cannot blindly assume fixture-only cascade is sufficient.

Canonical sub-phase analysis (analysis/2026-05-10-fgap-026-closure-sub-phase-structure.md) updated: Phase 7 row retired as separate stage; Phase 1.2 C.* sections redefined as per-package atomic units encompassing all 3 gates. Phase 8 (comprehensive fixture sweep) retained as Phase-8 territory only for cross-package consistency review post-atomic-unit-landings.

Pre-dispatch explore briefs for any future consumer-package cascade include: production source `.project` literal grep + absent-substrate-handling enumeration + per-test-file transitive-reach registry (already partially handled by scripts/orchestrator/extract-test-import-chains.ts; needs extension to enumerate gate-2 surface).

Plan-mode briefs for hard-throw primitive landings (current FGAP-026 + future analogues) declare the per-package atomic boundary as required reading; subagent briefs include the 3-gate enumeration as binding spec.

DEC-0015's 'NO hardcoded substrate-dir paths anywhere in pi-context, pi-jit-agents, pi-workflows, pi-behavior-monitors source' is the canonical principle; DEC-0021 operationalizes WHEN that cascade lands per consumer package (atomically with that package's other gates), not WHERE.

scripts/orchestrator/extract-test-import-chains.ts (per DEC-0019) gains an extension to enumerate not just test-import chains but also production-source hardcoded-literal sites + absent-substrate-handling code paths per package — pre-dispatch atomic audit surface.

decisions.title: Features schema description drops "Epic-level" qualifier; canonical kind name is "feature"
decisions.context: PM-vocabulary survey (analysis/2026-05-05-pm-vocabulary-prior-art-survey.md) catalogues "epic" as Scrum/SAFe-origin emphasis-on-scale qualifier. User direction 2026-05-14: "epic" reads as zero-signal. The features.schema.json description prose carried "L3 Work layer. Epic-level features with..." which conflated PM-jargon scale-emphasis with the canonical block name. The structural shape (feature → stories → tasks + scoped findings) is what carries meaning; the "epic-level" qualifier adds no machine semantics and reads opaque to non-PM-familiar readers.
decisions.decision: Drop "Epic-level" from features.schema.json description prose. Canonical kind name is "feature" (singular) / "features" (block array_key). config.block_kinds[].display_name will read "Features" (matching schema.title). No PM-scale qualifier in schema prose or display layer.
consequences: Schema prose reads cleanly to non-PM readers.
Display-name layer can rely on schema.title field directly (see DEC-0023).
Future block-kind authoring avoids PM-jargon qualifiers in descriptions.
No code paths or AJV behavior changes (description field is not enforced).

decisions.title: Schema title field is the authoritative source for block-kind display_name
decisions.context: Every .project/schemas/*.schema.json already carries title (e.g. "Framework Gaps" / "Design Decisions" / "Features") + description authored as canonical purpose statements. Authoring config.block_kinds[].display_name from scratch would duplicate this — and worse, drift if the two diverge. Schema title is authored once per block kind alongside required/properties/etc., already AJV-validated through normal schema-write surfaces. Config.block_kinds[].display_name role was ambiguous prior to this decision: amplify schema.title (duplicate) vs override (deviation only). User direction 2026-05-14 surfaced this in dialogue while reverse-engineering config draft.
decisions.decision: Schema title field is the canonical source for block-kind display_name. config.block_kinds[].display_name is override-only — populate ONLY when prose deviation from schema.title is needed for display context. Default rendering reads schema.title; UI/macros fall back to schema.title when config.block_kinds[].display_name absent. Same authority pattern extends to other block-kind metadata expressible in schema (description / title) before adding parallel config fields.
consequences: Reduces config.json authoring burden — 21 block_kinds[] entries don't each need display_name unless overriding.
Schema-side title edits propagate to display layer automatically.
Future display-name divergence requires explicit override entry in config; surfaces deviation as substrate.
Same principle extends to: schema.description → config.block_kinds[].description (if added); schema.properties[X].description → field-level display strings.
Phase 5 sub-phase 5.1 (config.json authoring) is reduced in scope — display_name layer mostly omittable.

decisions.title: Milestones are the primary declarative-organizing anchor; work cascades from + verifies upward to them; aspirational → satisfied → archived lifecycle
decisions.context: User direction 2026-05-14 dialogue articulating the substrate-canon model: a MILESTONE is the aspirational end-state-with-criterion+evidence_query authored declaratively at the top of a roadmap. Everything below it is decomposition: research (R-NNNN) explores what's needed; decisions (DEC-NNNN) settle choices; framework-gaps (FGAP-NNN) capture deficiencies blocking it; features (FEAT-NNN) scope coherent bodies of work toward it; phases group + sequence work-units; stories/tasks decompose features atomically down to atomic Task-1 execution. Verifications (VER-NNN) accumulate upward; when milestone.evidence_query satisfies against VER items, milestone moves from aspirational → satisfied (project history). Milestone is what reveals + organizes the work needing to be done. This decision crystallizes the canon already implicit in roadmap.schema.json + roadmap-plan.ts + DEC-0017 work-unit context composition.
decisions.decision: Milestones are the primary declarative-organizing anchor of work in pi-context substrate. Milestone authoring is the entry point to a body of work — the milestone names the aspirational end-state + evidence_query that defines satisfaction. All work-altitude blocks (research / decisions / framework-gaps / features / stories / phases / tasks) cascade from milestone authoring as decomposition: a milestone's evidence_query implies what work-units must produce evidence; authoring those work-units is the decomposition. Milestones bear lifecycle: aspirational/planned → active (work in progress) → satisfied (evidence_query passes; becomes project history) → archived. Roadmap is the named, status-bearing curation of an ordered milestone set; once milestones + phases are top-level blocks (FGAP-053 + FGAP-049 cascade), roadmap thins to {id, title, description, status} + lens-spec for grouping + closure-table edges for ordering. Forward verification (milestone evidence_query → satisfaction) is automatic via roadmap-plan; backward decomposition (milestone → required work-units) is human/LLM authored — framework provides scaffold, authoring fills.
consequences: Milestone elevation per FGAP-053 becomes higher-priority — milestone block-kind is the canonical entry surface for the substrate canon, not an inline roadmap detail.
Roadmap thins to envelope per FGAP-055 — substantive structure migrates to milestones + phases + relations.json.
Context-contract for unit_kind=milestone needed per FGAP-054 — gather-execution-context becomes the primitive for "what's the picture around MILESTONE-NNN".
Work-altitude tiers (FGAP-052 config.layers consumed-at-runtime gap) cascade from milestones: layer = altitude at which work-units sit; milestones organize across altitudes.
Forward query "is MILESTONE-NNN satisfied" works via existing roadmap-plan evidence_query primitive.
Backward query "what must happen before MILESTONE-NNN" works via walk-ancestors on relations.json edges once edges are authored.
Auto-decomposition (milestone → required tasks derivation) is NOT enabled by this decision; decomposition remains authoring work. Could be filed as separate framework-gap if desired.
Project goals (project.json goals[]) sit ABOVE milestones — multi-milestone arcs serve project goals; future cascade rule may make this explicit via project_goal_served_by_milestone relation_type.

references.label: Roadmap schema with milestones[] inline + evidence_query

decisions.title: pi-context substrate canon is vocabulary-neutral; user's block / layer / relation / lens / status conception bootstraps entirely from config + schemas + macros without code changes
decisions.context: User direction 2026-05-14 dialogue articulating the foundational extension goal: the entire substrate apparatus must be bootstrappable + enactable for a wholly different conception of blocks / layers / relations than what pi-context ships defaults for. The memory shape arises from user/LLM use of the tool and needs, not from hardcoded canonical vocabulary in source. This generalizes DEC-0015 (config drives substrate location) + DEC-0011 (schema-install opt-in) to their full extent: pi-context's source is vocabulary-machinery (block-api primitives + closure-table + lens infrastructure + query primitives + DispatchContext attestation + execution-context composition); the canonical vocabulary (block kinds / IDs / relation_types / lenses / layers / status) is config-driven user-authored data, not built-in commitment. Currently pi-context ships defaults (PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES list of 12 kinds; canonical macros for those kinds; etc.) which are USER-OPTIONAL per DEC-0011 install opt-in pattern but appear as hardcoded literals in source — partial alignment with the principle.
decisions.decision: pi-context substrate canon is vocabulary-neutral. The framework's source code MUST operate on canonical_id-keyed registries derived purely from config.json + .project/schemas/ + .pi/templates/ macros. Built-in defaults shipped in packages/pi-context/registry/ are user-OPTIONAL; a user with a wholly different substrate conception can clone pi-context, author their own config + schemas + macros + relation_types + lenses + layers, and have a fully-functioning substrate canon for THEIR vocabulary without modifying any pi-context source. Implications: (a) hardcoded vocabulary literals in source (e.g. PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES const) are gaps to close — vocabulary derives from loaded config; (b) macro library must scaffold user-added block kinds, not assume canonical-kind exclusivity; (c) lens-validator dispatch must be config-registerable, not code-registered; (d) layer registry must be runtime-honored from config.layers[], not symbolic; (e) test suite must exercise pi-context against a fully-different substrate vocabulary as universalization proof.
consequences: PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES const removal — block kinds derive from loaded config (FGAP-056 filed).
Macro library bootstrap surface for user-added kinds — drop-in macros + canonical-kind defaults + framework auto-discovery (FGAP-057 filed; cross-decision with FGAP-037).
Universalization test suite — pi-context exercises against a fully-different substrate vocabulary (FGAP-058 filed); proves the claim.
Lens-validator dispatch config-registration (covered by FGAP-052 already filed).
Runtime layer-aware behavior (covered by FGAP-052).

All future pi-context surface additions checked against vocabulary-neutrality: any new code path that hardcodes a block-kind name / relation_type literal / lens id / layer label is a regression and gets filed as a gap.
Documentation: 'bring-your-own-substrate' workflow needs explicit documentation in CLAUDE.md + skill-narratives + README — user can adopt pi-context with non-default vocabulary without code changes.

Implication for the milestone-as-anchor model (DEC-0024): even milestones are user-optional; a user-defined substrate could anchor on different concepts (initiatives / OKRs / projects / etc.); pi-context provides milestone-block-kind as a recommended default, not as a framework-mandate.

decisions.title: ID-prefix padding convention — 3-digit vs 4-digit zero-padding across block kinds
decisions.context: Inspection of current .project/*.json items surfaces concrete drift: R- (research) + DEC- (decisions) use 4-digit zero-padding (R-0001 / DEC-0001..0025); FGAP- (3-digit, 59 items, near-rollover at FGAP-100), TASK- (3-digit, 42 items), VER- (3-digit, 15 items), FEAT- (3-digit, 1 item), PLAN- (3-digit, 1 item), REVIEW- (3-digit, 1 item), CTX- (3-digit, 3 items), RAT- (3-digit, 8 items) all use 3-digit zero-padding. Drift surfaced via ls + node enumeration during 2026-05-14 reverse-engineered config draft (analysis/2026-05-14-step-1-config-draft-from-substrate.md). Existing items aren't broken — block-api operates on string ids regardless of padding — but lens binning by id-pattern + LLM ID parsing assumes uniform shape per prefix. Without canonical declaration, future authoring continues drift; future migration becomes harder per existing-id volume.
decisions.decision: 3-digit zero-padding (e.g. DEC-001, R-001) is canonical across ALL block kinds. The two outliers — R- (research, 4-digit, ~11 items) and DEC- (decisions, 4-digit, 50 items at enactment time) — migrate forward to 3-digit. Net migration scope: ~61 items (4-digit → 3-digit) versus ~170+ items that would have moved under 4-digit-canonical. The 999-id ceiling per block is adequate at current scale (largest 3-digit block today is issue- at ~91 items; second-largest FGAP- at ~113 items). Per-block-declared width (option 4) is explicitly rejected as 'accept the drift FGAP-048 was filed to fix' — it is the non-canonicalization, not a canonical choice. 4-digit-canonical is rejected by asymmetry: 170+ migrate vs 61.
consequences: Lens binning by id-pattern becomes uniform per prefix once canonicalized.
Existing items either preserve historical shape (per-block override) OR migrate forward.
relations.json edges (when populated) reference canonicalized ids.
LLM lookups + analysis MD authoring inherit the canonical pattern.

options_considered.label: 3-digit canonical + per-block historical exception
options_considered.description: Declare 3-digit canonical universally; R- and DEC- retain 4-digit as historical drift declared via config.block_kinds[].id_width override per block. No migration of existing items.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Drift remains visible as historical-not-canonical; per-block exception cataloged in config; future items per block follow declared width.

options_considered.label: 3-digit canonical + migrate R- and DEC- forward
options_considered.description: Declare 3-digit canonical; renumber R- (11 items, R-0001..0011 → R-001..011) + DEC- (25 items, DEC-0001..0025 → DEC-001..025). No leading-zero conflict.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Lowest-churn migration given current item counts; relations.json edges (when populated) need migration; analysis MDs + commit messages keep historical references but new authoring is canonical.

options_considered.label: 4-digit canonical + migrate 3-digit blocks forward
options_considered.description: Declare 4-digit canonical; renumber FGAP- (59) + TASK- (42) + VER- (15) + others.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Higher churn; accommodates higher per-block count without rollover (FGAP-9999 vs FGAP-999).

options_considered.label: Per-block-declared width with no canonical universal rule
options_considered.description: config.block_kinds[].id_width declares per-block width; framework respects per declaration; no canonical universal width.
options_considered.tradeoffs: No migration; persistent inconsistency; LLM lookups must consult config.block_kinds[].id_width before parsing.

decisions.title: ID-prefix casing convention — uppercase vs lowercase across block kinds
decisions.context: Single drift surfaced in 2026-05-14 substrate inspection: issue- (issues block) uses lowercase prefix while DEC- / FGAP- / TASK- / VER- / FEAT- / PLAN- / REVIEW- / CTX- / RAT- all use uppercase. issue- has 91 items (issue-001..091) — largest single-block id population. No technical force for either; visual + grep + cross-reference shape uniformity is the consideration. Issue ids appear in commit messages, analysis MDs, FGAP related_issues fields — every citation site cascades on this decision.
decisions.decision: Uppercase prefix is canonical across ALL block kinds. The single outlier — issue- (issues block, lowercase, 91 items) — migrates forward to ISSUE-. Direction follows by asymmetry: 12+ uppercase prefixes (DEC-, FGAP-, TASK-, VER-, FEAT-, PLAN-, REVIEW-, CTX-, RAT-, REC-, STORY-, REQ-, R-) versus 1 lowercase (issue-); migrating 1 to match 12+ is the obvious move, not the other direction. Per-block-declared casing (option 3) is rejected as 'accept the drift' — it is non-canonicalization. Lowercase-canonical is rejected by the same asymmetry.
consequences: Cross-block id parsing assumes uniform casing post-canonicalization.
Citations across analysis + commits + FGAP related_issues all settle on chosen casing.
If migration required, 91 issue items + every reference site needs sweep.

options_considered.label: Uppercase canonical + migrate issue- → ISSUE-
options_considered.description: Renumber 91 issues; every related_issues citation in FGAPs + analysis + commit references migrates. Uniform with all other block prefixes.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Largest renumber sweep in substrate so far; rg-grep across analysis + .project + commit messages + relations.json (when populated).

options_considered.label: Lowercase canonical + migrate all uppercase prefixes
options_considered.description: Inverse migration. Massive churn across DEC- (25) + FGAP- (59) + TASK- (42) + VER- (15) + others.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Inverted-uniformity at far higher cost.

options_considered.label: Per-block declared casing in config.block_kinds[].prefix preserves drift
options_considered.description: config.block_kinds[].prefix already carries the literal prefix string (e.g. 'issue-' vs 'DEC-'); no migration; framework respects per declaration.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Persistent visual inconsistency; LLM cross-block id-shape parsing must consult config not assume.

decisions.title: Phase block-kind shape — file-per-phase with integer discriminator vs array-block with PHASE-NNN string id
decisions.context: Two divergent shape declarations exist in source: (1) packages/pi-context/registry/schemas/phase.schema.json requires 'number: integer' as discriminator; file path is .project/phases/{number}.json (file-per-phase). (2) packages/pi-context/registry/schemas/roadmap.schema.json roadmap.phases[].id requires string matching ^PHASE-[A-Z0-9-]+$. Existing .project/phases/{1,2,3,4}.json files (pre-FGAP-026 planning artifacts: 'Make typed composition real' / typed agent specs / template inheritance / etc., status=planned, never closed) use integer discriminator. Code (roadmap-plan.test.ts) uses PHASE-A / PHASE-B / PHASE-C / PHASE-D single-letter suffix. LLM-filed commit body f3d6e41 (TASK-021..030 substrate authoring) uses PHASE-006..010 numeric-suffix zero-padded. No DEC binds them. Phase items don't appear in standard block-item enumeration; can't be filed via file-block-item.ts append surface (which expects array-block shape); resolve-items-by-id won't find PHASE-NNN unless special-cased. FGAP-049 + FGAP-053 (milestone elevation companion) both touch this question.
decisions.decision: ENACTED: phase block-kind is an array-block (single phases.json with phases[] array, items carrying PHASE-NNN string ids) — NOT file-per-phase-integer. The file-per-phase shape (phases/{number}.json, number:integer discriminator) is the pre-FGAP-026 legacy outlier. Array-block makes phases query-primitive-compatible (filter-block-items / walk-ancestors / find-references / resolve-items-by-id), edge-referenceable in relations.json (parent/child = PHASE-NNN), and consistent with milestone elevation (FGAP-053) under DEC-0013/0024/0025. Implementation: revise phase.schema.json to array-block; migrate buildIdIndex + ProjectState to read phases.json; convert legacy phases/{1..4}.json → PHASE-001..004 (status preserved); author PHASE entities for distinct tasks.phase values; migrate tasks.phase → task_positioned_in_phase edges; remove tasks.phase field. Closes FGAP-049.
consequences: Standard query primitives (filter-block-items / resolve-items-by-id / walk-ancestors / find-references) become applicable to phases post-canonicalization (currently invisible to them).
Lens projections referencing PHASE-NNN as bins resolve to phase items canonically.
Pre-existing phases/{1..4}.json files either retain integer slot OR migrate to PHASE-NNN ids depending on path chosen.
TASK-025 sub-phase 5.4 (phase.json authoring) gates on this decision.

options_considered.label: Migrate phase to array-block pattern (single phases.json with phases[] array, items with PHASE-NNN string id)
options_considered.description: Aligns phase with all other block kinds. Drop file-per-phase; consolidate into .project/phases.json (collide-name with directory) OR .project/phase.json. Migrate existing phases/{1..4}.json content into the array. Each item gets a PHASE-NNN id (string).
options_considered.tradeoffs: Most aligned with substrate canon (DEC-0013 + lens infrastructure); query primitives work canonically; existing 4 phases need conversion (low churn — pre-FGAP-026 content; status=planned never advanced).

options_considered.label: Retain file-per-phase + add PHASE-NNN id field bound to integer number
options_considered.description: phase.schema.json extends to require both number (integer file discriminator) AND id (PHASE-NNN string canonical), with binding rule e.g. PHASE-${number:03d} ↔ number. Roadmap.phases[].milestone references resolve via id. File paths stay phases/{number}.json.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Preserves existing files at low migration cost; query primitives still need special-casing per file-per-phase shape; persistent divergence from array-block pattern.

options_considered.label: Decouple entirely — phase block-kind has both shapes coexisting
options_considered.description: Roadmap-internal phases (inline arrays) are one entity; project-wide phase block (top-level) is another; both bear PHASE-NNN ids; binding only at the cite-site.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Maximum flexibility; maximum substrate duplication risk; resolution semantics ambiguous.

decisions.title: Substrate-dir replacement name — what user-chosen directory name replaces .project/
decisions.context: FGAP-026 closure (DEC-0015 enacted) requires user choice of substrate-dir name; current literal .project/ is the bootstrap default to be replaced. Resolver (resolveContextDir per Phase 1.1 / TASK-021 commit a98d) reads .pi-context.json bootstrap pointer at repo root, which carries the user-chosen directory name. /context init (Phase 6 / TASK-026) prompts for the name — no default to avoid hardcode-dressed-as-default per FGAP-026 callout. Migration ceremony (Phase 10 / TASK-030) renames this repo's existing .project/ → user-chosen dir + writes .pi-context.json. Cross-tool naming conventions for repo-local config dirs include: .git, .vscode, .claude, .pi, .gh, .npmrc — single-word leading-dot is common. Substrate-canon-dir naming considerations: brevity (frequent typing); semantic (what's inside); collision-risk (other tools); discoverability (alphabetical in ls -a). The substrate is project-context (matches package name pi-context); other candidates align with role: substrate / canon / arc / work / state.
decisions.decision: ENACTED: the canonical substrate directory is .context (matching pi-context package + the forthcoming /context subcommand surface). The current .project/ becomes the frozen ad-hoc source; .context/ is authored fresh + canonical via re-derivation (DEC-0036). The bootstrap pointer (.pi-context.json) + config.root flip to .context at cutover (FGAP-026 Phase 10); until then .project/ remains the active working dir.
consequences: Migration script renames .project/ → <chosen-name>/ in this repo (touches every block instance file path; relations.json edges; phase files; schema files).
/context init scaffold creates <chosen-name>/ + .pi-context.json bootstrap pointer for new projects.
All analysis MDs + commit messages + CLAUDE.md / HANDOFF.md / MEMORY.md references to .project/ migrate or carry historical-name notation.
Cross-package consumers (pi-jit-agents / pi-workflows / pi-behavior-monitors) inherit through the resolver — no per-package literal change.

decisions.title: Relation_type canonical_id naming shape — verb-based vs source_verb_target tripartite
decisions.context: DEC-0013 forces all inter-item relationships into closure-table edges with relation_type field; DEC-0017 declares per-unit-kind context contracts referencing relation_types. config.relation_types[] is the declared registry (currently empty in this repo). Existing test fixtures in packages/pi-context/src/roadmap-plan.test.ts use phase_depends_on + phase_member naming. LLM-filed substrate analysis (analysis/2026-05-14-step-2-resolution-patterns-from-substrate.md) inferred candidates from inline FK violations: task_in_phase / task_depends_on / decision_supersedes / decision_relates_to / decision_addresses_finding / decision_addresses_feature / decision_addresses_gap / gap_addressed_by_decision / gap_addressed_by_feature / gap_addressed_by_issue / feature_resolves_block / feature_depends_on / story_depends_on / finding_resolved_by / issue_resolved_by / rationale_supports_decision / requirement_depends_on / research_supersedes / review_finding_resolved_by / verification_verifies / plan_relates_to_decision / plan_phase_depends_on. Plus milestone-cascade types from DEC-0024 / FGAP-054: milestone_grounded_by_research / milestone_addressed_by_decision / milestone_blocked_by_gap / milestone_delivered_by_feature / phase_culminates_at_milestone / milestone_verified_by / milestone_depends_on / roadmap_contains_milestone / roadmap_contains_phase / project_goal_served_by_milestone. No DEC settles the naming shape.
decisions.decision: ENACTED: relation_type canonical_ids use tripartite source_verb_target snake_case (e.g. decision_supersedes_decision, phase_culminates_at_milestone, task_depends_on_task). Aligns with existing test fixtures (phase_depends_on / phase_member in roadmap-plan.test.ts); self-documenting; under DEC-0035 (canonical_id-permanent) the initial choice persists so clearest-wins. Bipartite loses source-context; kebab collides with kebab block-kind canonical_ids; verb_modifier is a half-measure. Edges store this canonical_id; config.relation_types[].display_name carries the mutable human label.
consequences: Every relation_type canonical_id authored in config.relation_types[] follows chosen shape.
relations.json edges (when populated) use these canonical_ids in their relation_type field.
context-contracts bundle_relation_types[] reference these names.
Lens specs (config.lenses[]) bin by these names.
DEC-0013 + DEC-0017 enforcement consistency depends on shape uniformity.

options_considered.label: <source>_<verb>_<target> tripartite snake_case
options_considered.description: e.g. milestone_depends_on_milestone / phase_culminates_at_milestone / decision_addresses_gap. Source + target both encoded in name. Direction implicit by position.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Maximum self-documenting; longer names; redundant when source/target obvious from context; aligns with existing test fixtures (phase_depends_on).

options_considered.label: <source>_<verb> bipartite (target inferred from edge.child)
options_considered.description: e.g. depends_on / supersedes / addresses / culminates_at / verified_by. Direction encoded only in edge.parent + edge.child. Names are pure verbs.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Concise; reusable across many source/target pairs (e.g. depends_on works for milestone→milestone, task→task, phase→phase); ambiguous when same verb has different semantics per source/target type; loses self-documenting source.

options_considered.label: <verb>_<modifier> shape
options_considered.description: e.g. depends_on / culminates_at / supersedes_with / verified_by_evidence. Verb-first; modifier optional disambiguator.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Hybrid approach; verb prominence; modifier carves out specific cases without source/target encoding.

options_considered.label: Hyphenated kebab-case <source>-<verb>-<target>
options_considered.description: e.g. milestone-depends-on-milestone / phase-culminates-at-milestone. Same as tripartite but kebab-cased.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Visually distinct from typical_snake_case identifiers; consistent with config canonical_id casing in other registries; may collide with kebab-cased block-kind canonical_ids (decisions / framework-gaps / spec-reviews).

decisions.title: Layer registry adoption — declare config.layers[] with L1..L5 substrate/query/composition/dispatch/surface labels
decisions.context: config.layers[] (declared in config.schema.json lines 25-38: id + display_name + description) is currently empty in this repo. FGAP entries use 'layer' field with values L1 / L2 / L3 / L4 (free-text, no validation against an installed registry). features.schema.json declares 'L3 Work layer' for features (per items.properties.layer enum L1..L5). PLAN-001 (.project/layer-plans.json) uses Muni L1-L5 restructure plans framework. Inferred layer set per analysis/gsd-2-foundational-intelligence.md + analysis/gsd-2-derivability.md + my conversational scaffolding: L1 substrate (block CRUD / schemas / closure-table primitives), L2 query (filter / resolve / walk / find-references), L3 composition (lenses / roadmap-plan / execution-context), L4 dispatch (jit-agents / workflows / monitors), L5 surface (commands / tools / ceremonies / display). FGAP-052 records the runtime-vs-declaration gap: even when declared, no code path consumes layers[]. Adopting the registry settles the labels canonically; consumption is downstream FGAP-052 work.
decisions.decision: Adopt L1..L5 substrate/query/composition/dispatch/surface as THIS repo's canonical layer registry — declared in config.layers[]. Already in widespread use as free-text across FGAPs (layer: L1/L2/L3/L4), features.schema.json (L3 Work layer enum), PLAN-001 layer-plans.json, and analysis docs. Maps directly to pi-context's own package layering: L1 substrate (block CRUD / schemas / closure-table primitives), L2 query (filter / resolve / walk / find-references), L3 composition (lenses / roadmap-plan / execution-context), L4 dispatch (jit-agents / workflows / monitors), L5 surface (commands / tools / ceremonies / display). The 'user-chosen scheme' option (option 2) remains valid for DOWNSTREAM adopters of pi-context — they declare whatever layer scheme fits their project; this enactment is THIS repo's dogfood declaration and does not foreclose other schemes for other adopters. 'Fewer layers (3-tier)' (option 3) rejected: the existing free-text usage already discriminates five layers and conflating any pair loses signal. 'Defer until FGAP-052 consumption lands' (option 4) rejected: declaration without consumption is still useful for validation; consumption is FGAP-052's separate work and not gated by this enactment.
consequences: Every block_kinds[].layer FK references resolve to declared layers[].id.
FGAP layer field validates against the registry (currently free-text).

decisions.title: Status-bucket normalization mapping — bridge per-block status enums to canonical buckets
decisions.context: Per current substrate observation across blocks: decisions enum = open / enacted / superseded; framework-gaps = identified / closed; tasks = planned / completed (+ schema permits in-progress / blocked / cancelled); verifications = passed (+ schema permits failed / skipped); issues = open / resolved; roadmap = draft / active / paused / complete / archived; phase = planned / in-progress / completed; research = complete / superseded. Three lifecycle families surface: (a) workflow-state (planned → in-progress → completed → blocked / cancelled — used by tasks / phase / features); (b) gate-state (open → closed; identified → closed — used by issues / framework-gaps); (c) authority-state (open → enacted → superseded — used by decisions / research with supersedes lineage); plus authoring-state (draft → active → paused → complete → archived — used by roadmap). config.status_buckets (declared in config.schema.json as additionalProperties enum complete / in_progress / blocked / todo / unknown) is the bridge: maps any raw status string → bucket, enabling cross-block status rollup. Currently config.status_buckets empty in this repo. FGAP-021 records the reconciliation gap.
decisions.decision: OPEN: status-bucket normalization mapping. Mandate-compliant options below.
consequences: Cross-block 'is X complete?' queries become uniform regardless of source block's enum vocabulary.
Lens binning by status uses the bucket layer rather than per-block enum literal.
roadmap-plan + future status-aware projection primitives consume the bucket map.
FGAP-021 closes once mapping declared + consumed.

options_considered.description: config.status_buckets carries every observed enum value mapped to bucket: { 'open': 'todo', 'enacted': 'complete', 'superseded': 'complete', 'identified': 'todo', 'closed': 'complete', 'planned': 'todo', 'in-progress': 'in_progress', 'completed': 'complete', 'blocked': 'blocked', 'cancelled': 'complete', 'passed': 'complete', 'failed': 'blocked', 'skipped': 'unknown', 'resolved': 'complete', 'draft': 'todo', 'active': 'in_progress', 'paused': 'blocked', 'complete': 'complete', 'archived': 'complete', 'aspirational': 'todo', 'satisfied': 'complete' }. Every enum value maps explicitly.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Comprehensive; explicit; requires re-curation when new enum values added; one config map shared across all blocks (collision risk on reused enum strings — e.g. 'open' means todo across decisions/issues but does it mean the same in another future block?).

options_considered.label: Per-block scoped mapping
options_considered.description: config.status_buckets becomes nested: { 'decisions': { 'open': 'todo', ... }, 'tasks': { ... }, 'issues': { ... } }. Each block has its own enum-to-bucket map.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Resolves collision risk; more verbose; matches authoring-locality of enums per schema.

options_considered.label: Schema-declared bucket field per status enum value
options_considered.description: Each block schema's status enum field declares bucket inline: enum: ['planned', 'completed'], x-bucket: { 'planned': 'todo', 'completed': 'complete' }. Config.status_buckets retired; bucket lives with the schema.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Bucket co-located with enum declaration; schema migration required for every block; AJV doesn't validate the x-bucket extension by default.

options_considered.label: Adopt all default observable enums uniformly with global flat map
options_considered.description: Global flat map per option (a); accept collision risk by ensuring 'open' / 'closed' / 'planned' / etc. always mean the same thing across blocks (enforce at schema-write time).
options_considered.tradeoffs: Simplest; requires discipline in schema authoring; if a future block uses 'open' to mean something else, it's a violation of the canon, not a config issue.

decisions.title: Conventions block schema migration — add facets needed for relational/lens/lookup discoverability
decisions.context: FGAP-051 records that conventions block schema (.project/schemas/conventions.schema.json) is pre-DEC-0013 / pre-lens-infrastructure shape: items have id (freeform slug like 'esm' / 'tsc-build' / 'no-pi-dir') + description (single payload) + enforcement (lint/test/review/manual) + severity (error/warning/info). Missing facets: title separate from description; category / domain (substrate-filing / naming / commit / build / monitoring); applies_to (target surface — substrate / source / tests / commit / specific-package); related_decisions (closure-table edge home); status / lifecycle (active / retired / superseded); source / citation (file path or DEC enacted from); canonical_vocabulary (engineering terminology). 16 existing rules need migration. Same shape questions apply to architecture / domain / requirements (zero-item; authored pre-relational-layer).
decisions.decision: OPEN: conventions schema migration scope + sequencing. Mandate-compliant options below.
consequences: LLM lookup of 'how to file substrate' / 'what build commands' / 'what naming convention' becomes filter-block-items predicate-driven, not grep-on-description.
Convention rules emerging from DECs gain closure-table edge home (relations.json with relation_type=convention_emerges_from_decision).
Lens binning by category becomes possible.
Status-tracked rule lifecycle records when rules retire.
Cascade: same migration applicable to architecture / domain / requirements schemas if they remain in current shape.

options_considered.label: Migrate conventions schema in single sweep + populate all 16 existing rules
options_considered.description: Schema-write update conventions.schema.json adding fields (title / category / applies_to / related_decisions / status / source / canonical_vocabulary); migrate all 16 rules to populate new fields with values authored from rule context. Simultaneously audit + propose-migrations for architecture / domain / requirements schemas.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Single coherent migration; larger scope; closes FGAP-051 atomically + surfaces sister-schema gaps.

options_considered.label: Migrate schema only; populate fields lazy per future authoring
options_considered.description: Update schema with new fields (all optional except title); existing 16 rules left as-is until each touched; new rules require new fields; schema-version migration handles default-population for existing.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Minimal immediate churn; persistent partial-state; LLM lookups partially work depending on which rules migrated.

options_considered.label: Author parallel block kind 'rules' with the desired facet shape; deprecate conventions
options_considered.description: Don't migrate existing conventions schema; author new 'rules' or 'rule-set' block kind with full facets; migrate convention items into new block; mark conventions block as deprecated.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Cleaner schema-design without legacy-shape constraint; substrate carries deprecated block until full migration; all consumers of conventions block need update.

options_considered.label: Audit + migrate conventions + architecture + domain + requirements as one work-unit
options_considered.description: Treat all four pre-relational-layer schemas as a coherent migration scope; one DEC enacts; one sweep populates.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Largest scope; addresses cascade comprehensively; aligns with mandate-007 (no deferring discovered issues — sister-schema gaps surfaced in FGAP-051's last paragraph).
