Comparison: DEC schema with rich facets
Comparison: FGAP schema with rich facets

decisions.title: canonical_id is permanent (primary-key semantics); display_name is the relabel surface
decisions.context: Investigation 2026-05-17 confirmed via relations.schema.json + config.schema.json that the substrate already separates stable opaque identity from mutable label: relations.json edge.relation_type stores the registered relation_type canonical_id (not the label); config.relation_types[] carries canonical_id + display_name as separate fields; same pattern for config.block_kinds[] (canonical_id + display_name per DEC-0023). This means human-facing relabeling is free — edit display_name, mtime-invalidated config re-read, zero edge migration. The migration-cost cases (FGAP-060) are ONLY canonical_id-itself changes. Without an explicit permanence discipline, LLMs/users may rename canonical_ids casually, triggering cross-file value-rename churn (relations.json edges + item ids embedded with prefix per DEC-0026/0027).
decisions.decision: ENACTED: canonical_ids carry primary-key semantics — set once, never renamed in normal operation. This applies to relation_type canonical_ids, block_kind canonical_ids, lens ids, layer ids, and item canonical_ids (DEC-NNNN / FGAP-NNN / etc.). All human-facing relabeling goes through display_name (config.*.display_name) + schema.title (DEC-0023), which are freely revisable as live data. Renaming a canonical_id is the rare, deliberate, migration-cost operation that the FGAP-060 renameCanonicalId engine exists to serve — not a routine edit. Consequence for vocabulary settlement (DEC-0026..0034): the INITIAL canonical_id choice matters because it persists; the display label does not lock in. This reduces most rename anxiety to a non-event (relabel via display_name) and isolates true migration to the deliberate canonical_id-change case.
consequences: Vocabulary relabeling (block-kind names, relation_type labels, lens labels, layer labels) is a live config.display_name edit — no migration, no rebuild.
renameCanonicalId (FGAP-060) is reserved for deliberate canonical_id changes (e.g. ID-prefix renumbering per DEC-0026/0027), not routine relabeling.
Initial canonical_id choices (DEC-0030 relation_type shape; DEC-0026/0027 prefix conventions) are the permanent decisions; their display_names remain revisable.
Schema/CLAUDE.md substrate-canon section gains the canonical_id-permanent rule so future authoring honors it.
Where a canonical_id MUST change, it routes through the FGAP-060 engine (edges + home-id + out-of-substrate report), never hand-edited.

references.label: DEC-0023 schema title as display_name authority
FGAP-060 value-rename engine this discipline scopes

decisions.title: Re-derive .context substrate clean via the config-based framework rather than in-place migrating .project
decisions.context: The FGAP-046/060/DEC-0028/FGAP-064 arc attempted in-place migration of the ad-hoc .project/ substrate (inline FK → edges, story/phase elevation, field strips). Apply attempts hit cascading schema-invariant collisions: tasks if-then (completed→verification required), decisions anyOf (≥1 forcing-artifact required), status-enum mismatches (feature done/todo vs tasks enum), dotted nested-task ids, prose citations of old ids. Root cause: some inline-FK fields encode INVARIANTS (decisions-cites-forcing-artifact; completed-task-has-verification), not just references; in-place stripping violates the schema while the field/constraint still exists, and relocating invariants to edge-validation must be coordinated with the strip. The .project/ substrate was authored ad-hoc BEFORE the config-based framework existed; retrofitting it through the framework's invariants is high-friction + accumulates migration scars. User direction 2026-05-17: name the canonical dir .context (DEC-0029) + keep .project as ad-hoc source + re-populate/refile into .context + refactor remaining extensions onto the config-based framework.
decisions.decision: ENACTED: finish the framework by RE-DERIVATION, not in-place migration. .project/ is frozen as the ad-hoc historical source (read-only reference). .context/ is authored fresh + entirely THROUGH the config-based canonical surfaces (config + schemas + block-api + relations + file-block-item), so every item conforms by construction: references authored as relations.json closure-table edges from the start (no inline-FK to strip); invariants satisfied at authoring time (forcing-artifact edges, completed-VER edges); elevations native (phase/story/milestone array-blocks). The in-place migration FGAPs (FGAP-046 inline-FK migration, FGAP-040 tasks.phase) DISSOLVE — they become 'author .context references as edges', a non-event under canonical authoring rather than a retrofit. Schema invariants relocate from inline-field-presence (AJV anyOf/if-then) to edge-validation (validateProject) in the .context-targeting schemas. The migration is dogfood: the canonical authoring surfaces PRODUCE the canonical substrate (FGAP-028). Cutover (FGAP-026 Phase 10) is a bootstrap-pointer flip + .project archive, not a data migration.
consequences: In-flight Phase 2 in-place inline-FK migration superseded; tmp/migrate-phase2.ts discardable (its logic informs re-population authoring).

Framework-machinery work stays real: validateProject edge-validation + relocated invariants; rename engine (FGAP-060); config-consumption (FGAP-052); macro library (FGAP-037/057); atomic-next (FGAP-059); PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES removal (FGAP-056); mixed-shape block_kinds (FGAP-061); empty-prefix (FGAP-062); milestone elevation (FGAP-053/054/055). Schemas authored edge-based.
Already-landed machinery valid + carried forward: config.json + relations.json schemas, phase array-block (DEC-0028), story schema (FGAP-064 schema), DEC-0030/0035 — all enable .context authoring.
Phase 2b substrate already written into .project/ (phase.json + edges + tasks.phase strip) stays as-is in the frozen ad-hoc dir; .context/ gets its own canonical phase data at re-population.
FGAP-046 + FGAP-040 reframed: closure = '.context references authored as edges' (re-population), not '.project stripped in place'.
.project/ preserved as point-in-time ad-hoc record (substrate-authorship-semantics).

Finishing sequence: (1) finish framework machinery + edge-based .context schemas; (2) re-populate .context by re-filing .project + analysis + git through canonical surfaces; (3) refactor extensions onto config-based framework (/project→/context rename + consumers); (4) cutover (pointer flip + archive).

decisions.title: Samples catalog identity — the package samples ARE this project's platonic packaged conception (dogfood)
decisions.context: During the unit-2.2 onboarding design dialogue (2026-05-20), a fresh-install UX trace revealed the package registry/ folder is misnamed and its role ambiguous (auto-installed default vs offerable catalog). Disentangling the word registry surfaced three concepts colliding on it: the package asset folder, the per-project config registries, and the menu of suggestions onboarding offers.
decisions.decision: The samples pi-context ships ARE this project's own platonic, packaged conception (decisions / tasks / framework-gaps / phase / story / ...), dogfooded. One authored artifact set serves triple duty: (a) this .context substrate, (b) the shipped samples catalog, (c) the onboarding accept-all default. Each sample is PACKAGED — schema + its relation_types + its invariants + a starter lens + description — not a bare schema file. The word registry denotes the config registries (block_kinds / relation_types / layers / lenses); the package registry/ folder IS the samples catalog and is renamed samples/. No separate generic abstraction layer is authored — the dogfooded platonic conception is the catalog.
consequences: Unit 2.2 is redefined: it authors the platonic PACKAGED conception (schema + relations + invariants + lens per kind), not schemas alone — its output IS the samples catalog.
registry/ -> samples/ rename; registry reserved for config registries.
Collapses the earlier installed-15 vs all-registry vs generic-defaults boundary fork: there is one platonic conception, dogfooded.
Every catalog sample must be clean / edge-based (DEC-0013) so a fresh adopter who accepts a sample inherits no inline-FK.
Pairs with the initProject defaults-imposition gap (FGAP-067): the catalog is offered, not auto-copied.

options_considered.label: Platonic dogfood catalog (chosen)
options_considered.description: Ship this project's own clean conception as the sample set; one artifact serves substrate + catalog + onboarding default.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Couples the shipped catalog to this project's conception; mitigated because pi-context dogfoods its own PM substrate.

options_considered.label: Separate generic abstraction layer
options_considered.description: Author a vocabulary-neutral generic sample set distinct from this project's substrate.
options_considered.rejected_reason: Duplicate authoring + drift between the generic set and the dogfooded substrate; contradicts the dogfood principle; no evidence a generic set differs usefully from the platonic one.

decisions.title: Onboarding model — /context init = standing incremental registry-population surfaces; accept-all | step-through
decisions.context: DEC-0011 (ship no defaults) is violated by initProject auto-copying defaults/ (FGAP-067), and a fresh adopter has no guided path to a conception (FGAP-066). The onboarding interaction shape was unarticulated; the unit-2.2 dialogue (2026-05-20) articulated it.
decisions.decision: Onboarding is a conversation that populates the config registries from the samples catalog (DEC-0037), conducted by the harness-confined main LLM via tools (the pi.sendMessage follow-up-turn pattern, DEC-0014-clean; lens-curate is the precedent). /context init is the FIRST PASS of standing, incrementally-invokable registry-population surfaces — the same surfaces later add a block kind, add a relation, or relabel — NOT a one-shot wizard; revisability is therefore structural. Two modes: accept-all (take the platonic bundle wholesale) and step-through (review / relabel / subset one registry entry at a time). The step-through UX is a bounded pi-sdk/TUI investigation, adopting canonical exemplars rather than inventing. Bespoke net-new block-kind authoring (a kind absent from the platonic set) is out of this scope — ship and subset the platonic conception first.
consequences: init = first invocation of permanent surfaces, so add/relabel later reuse the same machinery (no separate wizard).
accept-all is the trivial MVP onboarding; step-through is a separate UX-investigation track.
Depends on the samples discovery surface (FGAP-068) and the per-registry register/relabel write surfaces; the canonical_id-revise leg is the rename engine (FGAP-060).
Supersedes the initProject defaults-imposition (FGAP-067): onboarding offers, never imposes.
Bespoke-kind authoring deferred as a later, separate capability (not silently dropped).

options_considered.label: Standing incremental surfaces, init=first pass (chosen)
options_considered.description: One set of registry-population surfaces; init is just the empty-state entry; add/relabel reuse them.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Requires the surfaces to be CRUD-shaped from day one rather than init-only.

options_considered.label: One-shot init wizard
options_considered.description: A distinct guided wizard that runs once at setup.
options_considered.rejected_reason: Makes revisability a bolt-on; duplicates logic between init and later edits; the user rejected optional/one-shot framing.

decisions.title: No-optional registries — absence = degenerate default (layers empty = 1 implicit layer)
decisions.context: The unit-2.2 onboarding articulation (2026-05-20) surfaced that treating substrate registries as optional yes/no choices branches both the elicitation flow and the runtime semantics. Layers in particular is resolved INTO (block_kinds[].layer is an FK), so it cannot be genuinely absent without breaking layer grouping.
decisions.decision: Substrate registries are never optional choices. Each has a canonical empty-state: layers empty = ONE implicit layer that all block kinds belong to — the single legitimate degenerate default, because block_kinds[].layer must resolve. All other registries empty = genuine none (relation_types / lenses / invariants / hierarchy / naming): empty is a valid state meaning none, with no invented value. Onboarding presents defaults and lets the user elaborate; it never poses optionality (yes/no) gates. status_buckets currently carries a hardcoded default vocabulary — that is a separate DEC-0025 smell (FGAP-069), NOT a sanctioned degenerate default.
consequences: Layers consumption (FGAP-052) implements no-layers = 1-implicit-layer; until then layers is inert.
Config optional-markers are read as defaulted-semantics, not absent: only layers gets an implicit value; the rest are empty-tolerant arrays.
Onboarding elicitation is flat (present-default-or-elaborate per registry), not a yes/no decision tree.
status_buckets source-shipped default vocabulary is reclassified as a smell to fix (FGAP-069), not a blessed degenerate default.

options_considered.label: No-optional + degenerate default (chosen)
options_considered.description: Every registry always has a value; absence is a canonical empty-state (layers=1 implicit layer; others=none).
options_considered.tradeoffs: Requires defining the empty-state semantic per registry (trivial for all but layers).

options_considered.label: Optional registries with yes/no gates
options_considered.description: Onboarding asks do you want layers/lenses/etc.; absent means the feature is off.
options_considered.rejected_reason: Branches flow + semantics; layers cannot be truly absent (FK resolves into it); user rejected optional framing.

decisions.title: Substrate is the single source of truth for process + state; state is derived, never hand-stored; all other surfaces are projections or raw logs
decisions.context: Project goal (user, 2026-05-21): process + state must be EXACTLY preserved for zero-loss persistence. An audit this session found state hand-stored + drifted across six surfaces — substrate TASK/VER (TASK-043 in-progress for a superseded approach, TASK-044 planned for delivered work, units 2.1b/2.2/2.3 with no task at all), MEMORY.md Current Focus (pre-DEC-0036 framing AND 1.55x over its size limit so it loads truncated), the Claude Code task list (stale pre-pivot pending layer), the plan file ACTIVE marker, with HANDOFF.md the lone current one. N independent mutable state-stores guarantee drift; drift is loss. Root cause: the substrate was treated as one store among many rather than THE source with derived views.
decisions.decision: The .context substrate (typed blocks + relations) is the SINGLE authoritative source for both PROCESS (decisions, gaps, units-of-work, verification, reasoning) and STATE (current focus, status, next-action, in-flight, blocked). State is DERIVED from the substrate as a pure function of its contents; it is never independently hand-stored. All other surfaces are one of two kinds and neither is an authoritative independent state store: (a) GENERATED PROJECTIONS of the substrate (MEMORY.md Current Focus, HANDOFF.md, the Claude Code task mirror, plan ACTIVE) or (b) RAW APPEND-ONLY LOGS (the session transcript via claude-history). Hand-maintained duplication of state across surfaces is forbidden. Zero-loss has two senses that this split satisfies: raw exact preservation = the transcript (claude-history); usable reconstruction = the substrate (distilled process + derived state); together they are exact AND usable, recoverable without the live conversation. Where derivation/projection machinery does not yet exist, the projection surfaces are kept manually current as an explicitly drift-prone INTERIM, not as canon-blessed independent stores. [Amendment 2026-05-21 — un-conflate the EXTENSION vs the CLAUDE CODE HARNESS]: precise three-layer scoping. (1) The EXTENSION (pi-context, loaded by the Pi runtime) owns the substrate + its capabilities (file / query / derive currentState / validate). (2) Those capabilities are EXPOSED via the dual surface (DEC-0019/0020) + DEC-0016 to BOTH consumers — the Pi runtime (Pi tools, in-pi agents) AND the Claude Code harness (scripts/orchestrator/* + the `pi -p` bridge); the Claude Code instance drives the extension (filing, querying, currentState) exactly as this session has done. (3) The Claude Code harness's OWN working-memory artifacts (MEMORY.md, HANDOFF.md, the Claude Code task list, plan files) are HARNESS-OWNED — a separate consumer layer, NOT extension-managed projections; the extension never writes them. CORRECTION to clause (a) above: those harness files are NOT 'generated projections of the substrate' the extension produces — they are Claude Code harness working memory, kept current by the HARNESS APPLYING the exposed currentState capability to its own files. 'Single source of truth' scopes to the SUBSTRATE (the project's process + state); the harness files are downstream consumers, outside the source-of-truth model. The operative boundary: the extension EXPOSES derivation (use it from the Claude Code harness, like filing) which is intended and canonical, vs the extension WRITING the harness's files (it does not).
consequences: MEMORY.md / HANDOFF / Claude-task list / plan ACTIVE are downgraded from authoritative to derived-projection (or raw-log); none may hold state the substrate does not.

Every unit-of-work captures its process as substrate items (TASK + VER + any DEC) per DEC-0018/0020 — the transcript is the raw backstop, not the only home; units landing as commits-only is a violation.
Requires a full-state derivation + projection engine (FGAP-072) and unit-closure enforcement + cross-block status-drift validation (FGAP-073).
DEC-0018 (verify per step) + DEC-0020 (units produce typed substrate blocks) are reclassified from aspirational to ENFORCED; their violation is substrate loss.
Until the engine lands, hand-syncing the projection surfaces is recovery work (restoring lost state), explicitly recognized as the interim cost the engine eliminates.
Extends/anchors the DEC-0036 re-derivation: .context is built so state is derivable from line one.

Extension capabilities (file/query/derive/validate) are exposed + usable from the Claude Code harness (scripts/orchestrator/* + pi -p) per DEC-0016/0019/0020 — the un-conflation does NOT wall the harness off from the extension; it only stops the extension from writing harness-owned files.

MEMORY.md / HANDOFF.md / Claude Code task list / plan files are Claude Code HARNESS-owned consumers, refreshed by the harness APPLYING the exposed currentState; the extension neither produces nor writes them (corrects the earlier 'generated projections of the substrate' framing).

options_considered.label: Single substrate source + derived projections (chosen)
options_considered.description: Substrate is the only state store; all other surfaces are generated from it or are raw logs.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Requires building derivation + projection machinery (FGAP-072/073) before the drift fully stops; interim hand-sync remains.

options_considered.label: Disciplined manual multi-surface sync
options_considered.description: Keep the six surfaces, sync them by hand every step.
options_considered.rejected_reason: Proven to fail this very session — six surfaces drifted despite intent; N mutable stores drift by construction; cannot be zero-loss.

decisions.title: config.root is an OPTIONAL override; the .pi-context.json pointer is the substrate-dir SoT; the packaged conception ships no root
decisions.context: DEC-0015 mandates no default substrate dir (resolveContextDir hard-throws; /project init requires an explicit dir). But config.schema.json required 'root' and the packaged conception (samples/conception.json:3) shipped root: '.project' — a hidden shipped substrate-dir default (FGAP-094). adoptConception (project-context.ts:436) already overwrites conception.root with path.relative(cwd, contextDirAbs) on every adopted config, and projectRoot (project-context.ts:250) already falls back to resolveContextDir(cwd) — the .pi-context.json bootstrap pointer — when root is absent. config.root is therefore redundant with the pointer today. loadConfig/writeConfig validate against the bundled schema only (bundledSchemaPath('config')); config is not wired through validateBlockWithMigration, so a schema version bump triggers no migration. The forcing question: should the config contract keep 'root' required (and so force the template to ship a concrete default), or make it an optional override so the conception ships no root?
decisions.decision: Make config.root an OPTIONAL schema field. The .pi-context.json bootstrap pointer is the single source of truth for the substrate-dir name; config.root is an optional relocation override, set by adoptConception at accept-all from the init-chosen dir, and resolved-via-pointer when absent. The packaged conception is a TEMPLATE and ships no root. This enforces DEC-0015 on the shipped artifact: no default substrate dir is shipped, hidden or otherwise. config.schema.json version bumps 1.3.0 -> 1.4.0. User-story (affirmed by user authority at enactment): I name my substrate dir once; the framework honors that name everywhere and ships no competing default.
consequences: config.schema.json required[] drops 'root'; root description rewritten to optional-override-resolved-via-pointer; version 1.4.0
samples/conception.json ships no root key; samplesCatalog() / SamplesCatalog no longer surface a root
Hand-authored configs may omit root; projectRoot resolves the substrate via the pointer (already the behavior — now contractually allowed)
Relaxation only: existing configs that carry root remain valid; no migration needed (config not migration-wired)
The frozen .project/schemas/config.schema.json copy is unchanged (DEC-0036) and is not used by loadConfig (bundled schema is)
Does not rename .project anywhere — the residual 'project' naming/location debt remains FGAP-074 / DEC-0036 cutover territory

decisions.title: /context start is the sole human bootstrap entry — bootstrap human-only; operational verbs twinned + READY-gated; init/accept-all/install dissolve into start (impossibilization-by-design)
decisions.context: Substrate bootstrap is today a user-sequenced 3-step DAG: /project init <dir> (pointer+dirs) -> /project accept-all (config from the conception) -> /project install (materialize declared assets). Each step consumes the prior's output, so wrong order has nothing to read and misfires — FGAP-093 (running install before accept-all emits an error that even misnames the remedy). DEC-0038 (enacted) set the onboarding MODEL — standing, incrementally-invokable registry-population surfaces; two modes accept-all|step-through; conducted by the harness-confined LLM; explicitly NOT a one-shot wizard; offer-don't-impose — but it did not fix the ENTRY SHAPE that lets a user run the steps out of order. Two bootstrap inputs are irreducibly human: the substrate dir name (DEC-0015 forbids a default) and accept-all vs step-through (DEC-0038 offer-don't-impose). DEC-0014: the in-pi LLM operates via tools, not slash commands. DEC-0019/0020: every substrate op is dual-surfaced (library + Pi tool + CLI). pi/pi-tui SDK research (analysis/2026-05-23-pi-tui-onboarding-banner-research.md) confirms an extension-owned startup display slot (session_start + ctx.ui.setWidget/setEditorText) exists, while pi's exact update-banner slot is not extension-addressable. The forcing question: what entry-point shape makes out-of-order UNREPRESENTABLE while honoring offer-don't-impose and the irreducibly-human choices.
decisions.decision: Make /context start the single, always-safe, state-derived human entry point — the only bootstrap verb a user issues. It is impossibilization-by-design: there is no second user-sequenced step to misorder; the ordering lives inside start, derived from filesystem state (DEC-0040, nothing stored). (1) start is a STATE MACHINE that derives bootstrap state and falls through: NO_POINTER -> elicit dir name (suggest .context; never default — DEC-0015) then write pointer+dirs; NO_CONFIG -> elicit accept-all|step-through (DEC-0038 fork) then write config (accept-all=adoptConception; step-through=conversational curation, FGAP-066); NOT_INSTALLED -> materialize declared assets (idempotent); READY -> render the /context status view. Re-running start from any state resumes; it NEVER errors, even when already READY. (2) start does only what is mechanical for the user; it pauses solely for the two irreducibly-human CHOICES (dir name; adopt-mode). (3) start is HUMAN-ONLY: a slash command with NO tool twin — because both choices it gates on are human, the LLM cannot and must not create a substrate; in a fresh repo every LLM tool hard-throws and the LLM's correct behavior is to surface 'run /context start' to the human. (4) init / accept-all / install DISSOLVE into start as internal phases — their standalone command forms AND their tool forms are removed (the project-init and project-accept-all tools are dropped); the underlying library fns (initProject/adoptConception/installProject) remain as start's internals. (5) Every OTHER /context subcommand (status, view, validate, validate-relations, roadmap-*, plus the block CRUD tools) stays TWINNED (command for the human, tool for the LLM) and is uniformly READY-GATED at the dispatch layer: unmet precondition does not run the handler; it emits the one unified remedy 'run /context start'. The gate is state-based, not history-based (no 'did you run start' flag). (6) The unset-substrate remedy is unified to a single string 'run /context start', surfaced at the command dispatch gate (to the human, with editor prefill) AND carried in the tool hard-throw (BootstrapNotFoundError) so the LLM relays the correct human action rather than a raw error. (7) status owns steady-state READ; the standing registry surfaces own steady-state MUTATION; start's READY branch is a thin pass-through that renders status — not a second home. (8) The extension startup slot shows 'pi-context installed — run /context start' only while not-READY (session_start + ctx.ui.setWidget), then goes quiet. Extends DEC-0038 (does not supersede it); the conversational step-through remains FGAP-066.
consequences: NEW: /context start human-only command — state-machine conductor; precondition-free; never errors.

REMOVED from the surface: standalone init/accept-all/install commands AND the project-init + project-accept-all tools (bootstrap is human-only, folded into start). Library fns initProject/adoptConception/installProject retained as start internals.
ALL other /context subcommands twinned (command+tool) and READY-gated at the dispatch layer; one unified redirect 'run /context start' on unmet precondition.
Unset-substrate remedy unified: BootstrapNotFoundError + command gate both name 'run /context start'; the tool throw carries the guidance so the LLM relays the human action.
SUBSUMES FGAP-093: the misdirecting install error cannot occur — install is not a user verb and there is no misorderable second command.
The in-pi LLM CANNOT bootstrap a substrate (intentional, not a gap): the two gating choices are human; the LLM surfaces 'run /context start'.
status remains the steady-state read home; start's READY branch renders status (single projection, no duplicate home).
Startup slot (session_start + ctx.ui.setWidget) shows the start hint only while not-READY; aimed at the human actor.
Realizes the not-yet-built half of DEC-0038; the conversational step-through branch is FGAP-066; spec'd as FGAP-095.
Assumes the /context command surface (FGAP-074 / DEC-0036 step-4 cutover) — buildable on /project first then renamed, or after cutover; sequencing is the user's call.

decisions.title: Framework schemas (config/relations/roadmap/bootstrap) are bundled-validated, not installable catalog assets — installed_* enumerate block-kind assets only
decisions.context: Surfaced by FGAP-050 and forced by the FGAP-095 P1 runtime demo. The packaged conception (samples/conception.json) declared `config` and `relations` in installed_schemas, but installProject reports them notFound BY DESIGN — they are not block_kinds with catalog sources (install-subcommand.test.ts:131 'config and relations report notFound' asserts exactly this) — and loadConfig/loadRelations validate against the BUNDLED package schema (bundledSchemaPath in project-context.ts), never a substrate copy. So a fully + correctly installed substrate perpetually read not-installed: deriveBootstrapState could never reach `ready` (missing config, relations forever). FGAP-050's original premise — that config.schema.json must be installed into <root>/schemas/ for config.json to AJV-validate via the installed-schema resolution path — is contradicted by the current bundled-validation implementation. FGAP-095 P1 (deriveBootstrapState) is the first consumer that needs a crisp definition of 'fully installed', which FGAP-050/FGAP-087 left open. The forcing question: are framework schemas installable catalog assets or bundled-only?
decisions.decision: Framework schemas — config, relations, roadmap, bootstrap — are validated against the BUNDLED package copy (bundledSchemaPath) and are NOT installable catalog assets. config.installed_schemas / installed_blocks enumerate BLOCK-KIND assets ONLY: the user-vocabulary block schemas + starter block files installProject copies from the samples catalog. The packaged conception drops `config` + `relations` from installed_schemas (17 -> 15, symmetric with the 15 installed_blocks). 'Fully installed / ready' = config.json present AND all declared block-kind assets materialized; framework schemas require no substrate copy. No validation behavior changes — loadConfig/loadRelations already resolve the bundled schema; this ratifies that reality and makes the conception honest.
consequences: samples/conception.json installed_schemas drops config + relations (now 15, symmetric with installed_blocks).
installProject reports notFound=0 on the canonical conception; deriveBootstrapState `ready` becomes reachable + correctly defined — FGAP-095 P1 unblocked.
Closes FGAP-050: its 'install the framework schemas into <root>/schemas/' premise is superseded by bundled validation.

Gives FGAP-087 a clean catalog contract: framework-shaped schemas are not catalog entries; they are bundled (config/relations/bootstrap) or PM-lens framework (roadmap) — not 'a samples/ home to find'.
FGAP-091 (no install tool) is separately superseded by DEC-0042 (install is a start phase, not a tool) — re-scope independently; not on this decision's path.
installed_schemas/installed_blocks semantics are now crisp: block-kind catalog assets only; a name in installed_schemas that is not a block_kind is a misdeclaration.

decisions.title: Agent-as-tool dispatch home — a NEW dedicated pi-agent-dispatch extension whose ONLY scope is the sub-agent→sibling-agent pi.registerTool surface; jit-agents stays library imported directly by orchestrator + workflows + monitors
decisions.context: FEAT-004 (agents-as-tools, cluster-E delegation) needs a Pi tool that exposes 'invoke another agent as a tool' to sub-agents executing inside pi -p subprocesses. pi-jit-agents is canonically a LIBRARY per CLAUDE.md package table ('Library (not extension)') + docs/planning/jit-agents-spec.md (four boundary surfaces: load/compile/execute/introspect) and is tool-less by design (no pi.registerTool in packages/pi-jit-agents/src — verified). Three consumers per DEC-0049 uniform-agent axiom (orchestrator + pi-workflows + pi-behavior-monitors) need agent dispatch; all three consume jit-agents AS A SHARED LIBRARY via direct npm import (TASK-080 is establishing this for workflows + monitors right now, repointing monitor index.ts:25-27 imports off pi-workflows' duplicate onto jit-agents canonical). JI-021 user verbatim 'orchestrator uses jit-agents directly' is honored by all three consumers calling jit-agents library functions directly — the orchestrator's own use is NOT mediated by any extension. The remaining question is narrow: where does pi.registerTool('call-agent', …) — the surface that exposes agent-as-tool dispatch to a SUB-AGENT executing inside a subprocess — live. This decision answers that narrow question only.
decisions.decision: ENACTED (2026-05-26, narrowed 2026-05-26 per FGAP-112 working-out): the agent-as-tool dispatch surface registers in a NEW dedicated pi-agent-dispatch extension whose ONLY scope is hosting the sub-agent→sibling-agent pi.registerTool site (FEAT-004) and the dispatch-boundary capability clamp (FEAT-005, parent-grant ⊆ child-grant enforcement). The extension does NOT wrap or intermediate orchestrator use of jit-agents. The extension does NOT re-expose jit-agents' library surfaces. Orchestrator + pi-workflows + pi-behavior-monitors continue to consume pi-jit-agents AS A SHARED LIBRARY via direct npm import for their own use of load/compile/execute/introspect — JI-021 ('orchestrator uses jit-agents directly') honored literally for orchestrator + JI-010 ('jit-agents owns everything between I have a spec and I have a typed result') honored as the library surface. The new extension exists EXCLUSIVELY for the sub-agent agent-as-tool case: a sub-agent (running inside pi -p, with pi-agent-dispatch loaded) invokes pi.callTool('call-agent', { spec, input, grant }) → implementation calls jit-agents' executeAgent() under the hood with parent-grant ⊆ child-grant clamp at the boundary. The extension is loaded peer-to-peer by any subprocess context whose sub-agents may invoke siblings — workflow subprocesses, monitor subprocesses, orchestrator if it ever runs agents that need agent-as-tool — symmetrically, not hierarchically. Eliminations (hard, not preference): pi-jit-agents-as-extension is OUT — violates library-not-extension canon + widens past four boundary surfaces. pi-context is OUT — package cycle (pi-jit-agents already depends on pi-context). pi-workflows hosting dispatch is OUT — recreates a directional monitors→workflows runtime-load edge precisely when TASK-080 dismantles the source-import edge of the same shape (monitor subprocesses would have to load pi-workflows to gain agent-as-tool capability, violating workflows=orchestration-only and DEC-0049 sibling-consumer symmetry). pi-behavior-monitors is OUT — wrong domain. Supersedes the prior wider 'wraps the pi-jit-agents library' framing of this decision (2026-05-26 enactment language); the narrowing does not change which package gets created or its position in the dependency graph — it sharpens its scope to the sub-agent registration site only.

options_considered.label: NEW pi-agent-dispatch extension, scoped ONLY to sub-agent agent-as-tool pi.registerTool site (CHOSEN, narrowed)
options_considered.description: Thin extension that registers exactly one tool: pi.registerTool('call-agent', …) calling jit-agents' executeAgent() with parent-grant ⊆ child-grant clamp. Does NOT wrap or intermediate jit-agents library use by orchestrator / workflows / monitors — those continue direct library import. Loaded peer-to-peer by any subprocess whose sub-agents may invoke siblings.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Cleanest separation; cycle-free; jit-agents stays pure library per its spec; agent-as-tool capability available symmetrically to all subprocess contexts (workflow steps + monitor classifies + future orchestrator-spawned subprocesses) without forcing any consumer to depend on another. Accepted cost: a new extension package + install/version surface — judged correct layering for a distinct concern (sub-agent dispatch site) versus folding into an existing extension whose domain it does not match.

options_considered.label: NEW agent-dispatch extension WRAPPING pi-jit-agents (prior framing, NARROWED AWAY FROM)
options_considered.description: Earlier framing of this same decision: the extension re-exposes jit-agents' library surfaces in addition to hosting the dispatch tool.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Single extension surface for everything agent-related.
options_considered.rejected_reason: Conflicts with JI-021 ('orchestrator uses jit-agents directly') and JI-010 ('jit-agents owns spec→typed-result library surface'). If the extension wraps library use, the orchestrator + workflows + monitors no longer use jit-agents directly — they use the wrapping extension. Three-consumer constraint + uniform-agent axiom resolve cleanly only when jit-agents is consumed as shared library directly and the extension's scope shrinks to the registration site alone. Narrowed 2026-05-26 per FGAP-112.

options_considered.label: pi-jit-agents grows extension surface (registers its own dispatch tool)
options_considered.description: Add pi.registerTool to pi-jit-agents itself; package becomes both library + extension.
options_considered.tradeoffs: No new package.
options_considered.rejected_reason: Violates CLAUDE.md package table 'Library (not extension)' declaration AND docs/planning/jit-agents-spec.md four-boundary-surfaces definition. Widens jit-agents' surface beyond its declared scope. Additionally awkward under multi-consumer constraint: workflows would call an extension tool from inside their own extension's step machinery when a library import suffices.

options_considered.label: pi-workflows hosts the pi.registerTool('call-agent', …)
options_considered.description: Fold the agent-as-tool registration into pi-workflows since workflows already consume jit-agents and run agent subprocesses.
options_considered.tradeoffs: No new package.
options_considered.rejected_reason: Forces monitor subprocesses to runtime-load pi-workflows to gain agent-as-tool capability, recreating a directional monitors→workflows runtime-load edge precisely when TASK-080 dismantles the source-import edge of the same shape (monitor index.ts:25-27 imports being deleted). Violates DEC-0048 (workflows=orchestration only) and DEC-0049 (uniform-agent axiom — consumers are siblings of jit-agents, not hierarchically dependent on each other for agent infrastructure).

options_considered.label: pi-context
options_considered.description: Register in the substrate tool hub.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Single discovery surface.
options_considered.rejected_reason: PACKAGE CYCLE — pi-jit-agents already depends on pi-context; pi-context importing pi-jit-agents (transitively, to call executeAgent) is circular. pi-context is the zero-@davidorex-dependency foundation. Hard-eliminated.

options_considered.label: pi-behavior-monitors
options_considered.description: Register alongside monitor tools.
options_considered.tradeoffs: No cycle (already imports jit-agents).
options_considered.rejected_reason: Wrong domain — monitoring, not agent dispatch. Same monitors→workflows-shaped objection in reverse if monitors hosted the surface: workflow subprocesses would load monitors to gain agent-as-tool.

consequences: Gates the detangle arc: FEAT-001 / TASK-080 (workflows + monitors consume jit-agents whole agent layer as shared library) → DEC-0044 narrowed (this) → FEAT-005 (capability composition + parent-superset child-tool clamp lands in the new pi-agent-dispatch extension's dispatch-tool implementation) → FEAT-004 (the dispatch tool itself ships in the new extension).

The FGAP-099 escalation guard (child tool surface ⊆ parent's) lands in the new extension's pi.registerTool('call-agent') implementation, enforced at the dispatch boundary — every consumer that loads the extension inherits the clamp identically. Per-caller clamps are forbidden (the gap is fixed at the registration site, not duplicated).

FGAP-099's prior workflow-local fix was reverted (commit 117b351) as wrong-layer; the FEAT-005-correct design (composeToolGrant / explicit-grant / empty-default / clamp) is preserved in history 7f8f991..7463138 for reuse at the new home.

Orchestrator + pi-workflows + pi-behavior-monitors source-import @davidorex/pi-jit-agents directly for their own load/compile/execute/introspect needs — no intermediary, no wrapping. JI-021 + JI-010 honored literally.

The new extension's exact package name (pi-agent-dispatch is a working name) and whether it joins the meta pi-project-workflows is a FEAT-004-build detail, not part of this home decision. Scope-narrowing supersedes the prior wider language; everything else in this decision stands.

references.label: pi-workflows owns dispatch (no standalone agent tool)

decisions.title: Substrate path resolution unifies on the .pi-context.json pointer (resolveContextDir); config.root is not a path-resolution input
decisions.context: FGAP-079: the schema/block file-path surface diverges — schemas/blocks are WRITTEN via projectRoot(cwd) (honors config.root) but READ via resolveContextDir(cwd) (pointer only). config.json + relations.json are pinned to resolveContextDir by NECESSITY (projectRoot must read config.json to learn config.root, so config.json cannot itself move). Therefore honoring config.root for blocks/schemas while config/relations stay at the pointer would SPLIT the substrate across two dirs — incoherent. config.root coincides with the pointer in practice (adoptConception sets config.root = relative(cwd, resolveContextDir)); the divergence is latent. The 'relocatable per config / GitHub issue #3' feature (projectRoot honoring config.root) is half-wired (writes honor it, reads don't, config/relations can't) and, fully wired, would only split the substrate. DEC-0041 already made the .pi-context.json pointer the substrate-dir SoT and config.root optional; this clarifies/extends it.
decisions.decision: Substrate path resolution unifies on the .pi-context.json pointer (resolveContextDir) for read + write, blocks + schemas + config + relations. config.root is NO LONGER a path-resolution input: projectRoot(cwd) drops its config.root branch and returns resolveContextDir(cwd), auto-unifying every write-side caller with the read side. config.root is retained as optional config DATA (DEC-0041) but does not affect resolution. Substrate relocation is via the pointer (a future /context migrate flips .pi-context.json's contextDir, moving the WHOLE substrate coherently — DEC-0036 step-5), not via config.root. Additionally, a single name validator (assertSubstrateName, /^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$/) guards every name->path builder so a traversal name (../, /, ..) is rejected on both read and write (now reachable from the in-pi write-schema tool, FGAP-077).
consequences: projectRoot returns resolveContextDir; schema + block read==write (the FGAP-079 divergence is gone for the whole surface, not just schemas).
config.root no longer relocates anything; the half-wired issue-#3 relocation feature is retired in favor of pointer-based relocation (coherent, whole-substrate).

Behavior-preserving in practice: adoptConception sets config.root == pointer, so projectRoot already returned the pointer dir; only the latent non-default-config.root case changes (now unified instead of split).
The project-context.test 'respects config.root override' test inverts: projectRoot now IGNORES config.root.
Path-traversal is rejected at every builder (read + write); valid block/schema names are [A-Za-z0-9_-]+ (block_kind canonical_ids).
config.schema.json root description should note root is optional metadata, not a path input (cosmetic follow-up). Extends DEC-0041; relates to DEC-0015 + DEC-0036 step-5 pointer-based relocation.

decisions.title: FGAP-074 /project->/context source-surface naming canon — unify identifiers on 'context'
decisions.context: FGAP-074 renames the framework's OWN source surface (the 3 project-*.ts modules + their subpath exports, exported identifiers, the 11 project-* Pi tools, the /project command) from pi-project-era 'project' naming to 'context'. C0 is the decision-only chunk that settles the target names so C1-C7 are pure mechanical codemod. The surface already converges on 'context' (package @davidorex/pi-context, command target /context per DEC-0042, the .context dir target, context-* tools, the already-context-named resolveContextDir/writeBootstrapPointer/schemaPath). Two naming questions were non-mechanical: (1) the project-context.ts target (context-context is absurd), and (2) whether the data-layer identifiers (getProjectContext/ProjectContext/validateProject + the State/Validation interface families) should adopt a precise second noun 'substrate' (already the codebase's PROSE term for the data layer) or stay in the 'context' vocabulary. Empirical grounding: ProjectState provably carries non-substrate fields (sourceFiles, sourceLines, testCount, lastCommit, recentCommits) so it is a whole-project derived dashboard, not a data-layer artifact; getProjectContext loads config+relations (the materialized data layer); validateProject checks referential integrity of blocks/relations. The 'Context' token is overloaded in-tree (DispatchContext x50, ContextBundle x20, resolveContextDir x50, contextBlocks).
decisions.decision: Unify ALL renamed source identifiers on the 'context' vocabulary; 'substrate' remains a prose/conceptual term only (CLAUDE.md keeps calling the data layer 'the substrate'), never a code identifier. Collision with the overloaded Context token is avoided by QUALIFIED interface names (ContextData, not bare Context). Canon: FILES/SUBPATHS: project-dir.ts->context-dir.ts (./context-dir), project-context.ts->context.ts (./context), project-sdk.ts->context-sdk.ts (./context-sdk). IDENTIFIERS: getProjectContext->loadContext; ProjectContext->ContextData; validateProject->validateContext; ProjectValidationResult/Issue->ContextValidationResult/Issue; projectState->contextState; ProjectState->ContextState; projectTemplatesDir->contextTemplatesDir; installProject->installContext; PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES->CONTEXT_BLOCK_TYPES. DROPS (not renamed): projectRoot + projectDir are collapsed into the canonical resolveContextDir (post-DEC-0045 both are no-op aliases that return resolveContextDir(cwd); call-sites repoint during C2-C4) — this also dissolves the projectDir->contextDir local-var collision hazard; PROJECT_DIR (@deprecated '.project' literal) is dropped outright (its cross-package consumers migrate off it in C3). TOOLS/COMMAND: project-* Pi tools -> context-*; /project -> /context; PROJECT_SUBCOMMANDS -> CONTEXT_SUBCOMMANDS; the DEC-0042 bootstrap trio (init/accept-all/install) renamed uniformly now (FGAP-095 P4 removes the then-context-named trio when /context start lands). RESERVED (DEC-0044/FEAT-004): keep a /context dispatch verb unclaimed for the future agent-dispatch tool — proposed 'run' (/context run); soft, finalized by DEC-0044; does not block C0.

options_considered.label: Unify on context (CHOSEN)
options_considered.description: All renamed identifiers in the context family; substrate stays prose-only; qualified interface names (ContextData/ContextValidationResult) dodge the DispatchContext/ContextBundle collision.
options_considered.tradeoffs: One vocabulary on the surface a consumer touches (package/command/dir/tools/imports all 'context'); the existing prose(substrate)/code(context) distinction does not worsen. Cost: 'context' carries more meaning, relies on qualifier discipline (never a bare Context interface).

options_considered.label: Split: substrate for the data layer, context for the surface
options_considered.description: getSubstrate/Substrate, validateSubstrate/SubstrateValidationResult for data-layer ops; contextState for the dashboard.
options_considered.tradeoffs: More precise denotation (data layer vs derived view); 'substrate' is already the documented term.
options_considered.rejected_reason: Introduces a SECOND noun into the import surface — the package called 'context' would expose 'substrate' functions, a code-vs-surface divergence the consumer hits on every import. Precision is recoverable via the verb (loadContext vs contextState vs validateContext) without a second noun. User decision 2026-05-23; revisit if problematic.

options_considered.label: Rename projectRoot/projectDir -> contextRoot/contextDir (instead of dropping)
options_considered.description: Keep the two wrappers under context names.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Mechanical.
options_considered.rejected_reason: Post-DEC-0045 both merely return resolveContextDir(cwd); renaming re-mints the redundancy DEC-0045 unified. Collapse to resolveContextDir is the coherent call.

consequences: C1-C7 become a pure mechanical codemod against this map; no further naming judgement needed downstream.
resolveContextDir is the SOLE substrate-dir-path resolver (projectRoot/projectDir gone); every former caller repoints to it during C2-C4.
PROJECT_DIR drop: pi-workflows (and any) bare-segment consumers must migrate off it in C3; C3/C7 greps must show zero survivors.

Qualified interface names (ContextData, ContextValidationResult/Issue, ContextState) are mandatory — a bare 'Context' interface is forbidden (would collide with DispatchContext/ContextBundle reading).
Subpath @davidorex/pi-context/context is mildly stutter-y but harmless; context.ts sits beside dispatch-context.ts/execution-context.ts as the core module.
/context run reserved for the FEAT-004 agent-dispatch tool (DEC-0044 finalizes the verb); C5 must not claim it.

Gates FGAP-074 C1-C7 and (transitively) FGAP-095 P2-P6. OUT of scope: package name @davidorex/pi-project-workflows, the .project dir literal (DEC-0036 step-5), user-substrate vocabulary (DEC-0025), already-context-named symbols.

Relates to DEC-0042 (/context command + bootstrap trio), DEC-0044 (reserved dispatch verb), DEC-0045 (projectRoot/projectDir collapse), DEC-0036 step-5 (dir-literal cutover, separate), DEC-0025 (user vocab, untouched). [DEC<->DEC edges deferred to TASK-065 post-.context cutover; recorded in prose here.]

decisions.title: Constitutional capability model for autonomous in-pi orchestration — config legislative, main-agent executive, validators judicial; capability mutation requires human ratification
decisions.context: Evaluation (2026-05-24) of whether the orchestrator->subagent operational pipeline (run this session by a Claude-Code orchestrator tasking subagents) is achievable by an extensions-tools-constrained in-pi agent using pi-context + pi-jit-agents. Finding: the declarative pipeline is ~85% expressible today (pi-workflows agent/command/gate/block/monitor/pause steps; shell IS available via command/gate steps; substrate authorship complete via Cluster A+B; introspect.agentContract ALREADY projects an AgentSpec to a tool shape). The genuinely missing pieces for ADAPTIVE self-orchestration are (1) agent-dispatch as a first-class tool (FEAT-004 / DEC-0044, open; pi-jit-agents is tool-less) and (2) operation-granular JIT capability composition + sandbox (FEAT-005). User articulated the governing model: a subagent is a programmatic action (a tool call carrying composed atomic-context chunks) that receives its OWN exactly-scoped tools+perms, JIT-composed from an empty-state registry (no ambient tools/perms; configs+schemas+macros materialize everything — generalizing DEC-0015/0011/0025). The human stays in the loop by AUTHORIZING the config and its mutations proposed by the main agent. Framed as a separation of powers ('constitutional').
decisions.decision: ENACTED (2026-05-26), in code terms (the prior legislative/executive/judicial framing was thinking-scaffolding; the model below is the operative form). No genuine fork remained at settlement — the model is determined by FEAT-005 + DEC-0014/0015/0011 + FGAP-102; settling = recording it. (1) A dispatched agent's tools are declared in its .agent.yaml spec (schema-shaped, macro/template-composed at the jit-agents compile boundary); default EMPTY (DEC-0015/0011 generalized) — an agent gets nothing it does not declare. (2) The grant is OPERATION-GRANULAR, not tool-wholesale (FEAT-005): a scoped right (run `npm test`; write path X) rather than `bash`/`write` entire. config + schemas + macros are the registry of what is grantable (default empty); the spec carries the per-invocation scoped grant drawn from it. (3) The dispatch path grants exactly the spec's declaration, CLAMPED to a subset of the dispatching parent's grant AT OPERATION SCOPE (FGAP-099, enforced at the DEC-0044 dispatch extension). A child can never exceed its parent; subagents are spec-blind recipients with no escalation path. (4) The orchestrator (DEC-0014: tools-only, no ambient bash/read/write/edit) is the SOLE spec/schema author. It may author work-orders/specs whose grants are within the existing registry (writer.kind=agent — allowed). WIDENING the registry (a new tool/op/capability bundle) is a capability mutation requiring HUMAN ratification: writes to capability/registry fields require writer.kind=human; agent-authored capability expansion is rejected at the block-api write chokepoint (no parallel ungated path). The existing DispatchContext WriterIdentity discriminant (human|agent|monitor|workflow) is the lever. (5) The TERMINAL acceptance verdict on any executable artifact (code or substrate change) is produced by RUNNING THE REAL DETERMINISTIC CHECKS the executive cannot fake — compiler/type-check/test exit code, schema validator, git diff, real runtime events — never an LLM self-assessment (FGAP-102; DEC-0018 is its executable form, inherited by autonomous loops per FEAT-006). An LLM may triage/route but is never the terminal gate. This is the governance layer over FEAT-004 (dispatch) + FEAT-005 (capability composition); homed at the DEC-0044 dispatch extension; enabled by DEC-0014. The only build-time residue is the literal JSON schema of a scoped grant (a FEAT-005 implementation detail, not a decision).

options_considered.label: Constitutional capability model (CHOSEN direction)
options_considered.description: Config legislative + main-agent executive + validators judicial; empty-state default; capability mutation human-ratified as a structural gate.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Makes self-orchestration TRUSTWORTHY (minimal, exact, sanctioned authority per subagent) rather than merely possible. Cost: requires building FEAT-004 dispatch-as-tool + FEAT-005 capability-composition/sandbox + a structural ratification gate; more upfront machinery than ad-hoc dispatch.

options_considered.label: Bare FEAT-004 dispatch without a capability layer
options_considered.description: Agents-as-tools with category-granular tool grants only (--tools read), no per-operation perms, no ratification gate.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Simpler; but lets a self-orchestrating main agent grant broad authority to subagents (edit source, run shell) without exact scoping or human sanction.
options_considered.rejected_reason: Necessary but not sufficient — unsafe for autonomous self-orchestration; the capability + constitutional layer is the property that makes it trustworthy.

consequences: Gates/shapes FEAT-004 (dispatch-as-tool) and FEAT-005 (JIT capability composition + sandbox) — both must land for the executive branch to function autonomously.
FGAP-063 (procedural-policy has no substrate block) is the likely legislative home for the process/capability canon — this DEC motivates closing it.

Converts human-authorization from behavioral norm (mandate-001 / substrate-authorship-semantics) to a structural gate on legislative (config/capability) mutation; the executive proposes, the human ratifies.

Builds on DEC-0014 (tools-only main thread — no ambient authority), DEC-0015/0011/0025 (empty-state config-driven), DEC-0019/0020 (dual-surface), DEC-0018 (judicial: runtime-demo+adversarial). introspect.agentContract already supplies the agent->tool-shape projection the executive needs.

The declarative pipeline remains expressible as a pi-workflow today; this model is what enables the ADAPTIVE (replan-on-finding) orchestration a static DAG cannot express, by giving the reasoning main agent safe dispatch + capability tools.

references.label: workflow shell-capable step types (command/gate) — pipeline expressibility

decisions.title: Existing workflows + bundled agents + their tests are NOT targets of work — disposable legacy PoCs predating pi-context/pi-jit-agents; the workflow FRAMEWORK is the only target
decisions.context: Recurring scope inversion (observed across the FGAP-099/FEAT-001 work): framework changes were being constrained or blocked by 'this breaks bundled workflow X / agent Y / test Z', and FEAT-001 STORY-006 treated bundled classifier/agent YAMLs as keep-and-align artifacts. The user grounded the project's actual intent (jit-agents user-intentions report analysis/2026-05-26-jit-agents-user-intentions.jsonl, JI-002: existing agents are development PoCs, recreatable from a correct framework) and issued an explicit scoping directive.
decisions.decision: User directive (2026-05-26, verbatim): "zero existing workflows are to be considered targets of any work. that goes for their tests too. they are nothing but legacy workflows development artifacts that predate pi-context and pi-jit-agents. the workflow framework is the only target." Operative: the existing .workflow.yaml specs, the bundled agents/*.agent.yaml + their templates/schemas, and the tests OF those legacy artifacts are NOT targets — not to be preserved, aligned, fixed, or treated as constraints/regression gates. They are disposable, recreatable from a correct framework. The ONLY targets of work are the workflow FRAMEWORK (the pi-workflows engine code) and the agent layer (pi-jit-agents). DISTINCTION: framework/engine UNIT tests (tests of pi-workflows/pi-jit-agents/pi-context engine behavior) ARE targets and remain regression gates; tests that exercise the legacy bundled workflows/agents are NOT.
consequences: A framework change that breaks a legacy bundled-workflow/agent test is not a regression — the artifact is disposable; do not contort framework work to keep legacy artifacts green.
Resolves the FEAT-001 STORY-006 keep-and-align-bundled-agents tension toward DISPOSABLE (aligns DEC-0048 with JI-002).
The FGAP-099/agent-layer concern that 'every bundled workflow agent breaks' under an agent-layer change is moot — bundled agents are not targets.
Agent-layer unification (FEAT-001) need not preserve bundled-agent layouts/resolution; legacy bundled agents conform to the corrected framework or are discarded.
Verification gates for framework work assert engine behavior, not legacy-artifact behavior.

decisions.title: Uniform-agent definition — 'an agent' is ONE abstraction (the jit-agent), used uniformly across all consumers (monitors, workflow steps, agents-as-tools); no per-consumer agent kinds
decisions.context: The intentions eval (analysis/2026-05-26-project-vs-jit-intentions-eval.jsonl, EV-013/JI-001) flagged the user's most foundational definitional intent — agent-as-one-thing — as NOT encoded as substrate canon (only implied by FEAT-001/DEC-0044). This DEC closes that gap directly by recording the axiom, so a substrate query (not a narrative read) surfaces it. Pairs with DEC-0048 (existing artifacts not targets / disposable — JI-002, the operational complement) and FEAT-001 (the implementation of the axiom).
decisions.decision: User-stated (JI-001, verbatim, 2026-04-09): "an 'agent' is the same thing whether it be used as a monitor or used in and by workflows." OPERATIVE: there is ONE agent abstraction — the jit-agent (pi-jit-agents library: AgentSpec + loader + compile/templates/macros/schemas/contextBlocks + capability composition + execute). Every consumer uses THIS abstraction: pi-behavior-monitors (monitor classifiers + steering subagents), pi-workflows (a workflow step that needs an agent dispatches a jit-agent), the agents-as-tools dispatch surface (FEAT-004 projects from introspect.agentContract over the same AgentSpec). There are no per-consumer agent kinds. Duplicates of the agent abstraction in consumer packages (e.g. pi-workflows' parallel AgentSpec + agent-spec loader + agent-template machinery + compileAgentSpec) are non-canon by construction and to be removed (FEAT-001). The disposability of existing artifacts (JI-002: 'all existing agents are development proofs of concept… recreatable from a correct framework') is recorded in DEC-0048.
consequences: FEAT-001 (pi-workflows + pi-behavior-monitors consume pi-jit-agents whole agent layer; delete duplicates) IS the implementation of this axiom; closes issue-043 duplicate-agent-infrastructure.
Any per-consumer agent abstraction (a separate monitor-agent / workflow-agent / dispatch-agent kind) is non-canon by construction; future PRs introducing one violate this DEC.
FEAT-004 (agents-as-tools dispatch surface) projects from the same AgentSpec via introspect.agentContract; it does NOT define a 'tool-agent' kind.
TASK-080 (re-scoped) inherits this axiom: 'pi-workflows is orchestration-only; a workflow step needing an agent dispatches a jit-agent directly' is the axiom's operational form.
DEC-0048 (existing artifacts disposable; framework is the only target) is the operational complement of this definitional axiom.

decisions.title: Adopt data-room three-artifact frame for decision-lifecycle substrate — split current .project/decisions.json into recognitions / decisions / substrate-conflicts blocks with uniform field tightening
decisions.context: The current single .project/decisions.json block conflates pre-decisions with actual decisions per FGAP-113 (filed 2026-05-26): block name carries colloquial 'has been decided' meaning while status enum admits 'open' for pre-decisions; the schema's own description stretches 'decision' stipulatively to cover the whole lifecycle but the word remains semantically misleading. The status enum (open|enacted|superseded) further collapses operationally-distinct sub-states inside 'open' (recognized / drafted / reviewed-and-revised) — fresh contexts cannot distinguish ready-to-enact from still-needs-framing. Empirical evidence at analysis/2026-05-26-decisions-block-shape-survey.md commit 4900a34: 49 entries surveyed; 20 emergent shape-clusters spanning heterogeneous writer-identity formats, enacted-tuple completeness variance, in-body revision-prose stacking (Amendment/Widening/narrowed), null-state convention variance, decision-as-question vs decision-as-answer. Data-room metaphor at /Users/david/Projects/data-room-pi-context.md establishes three first-class artifacts an orchestrator/LLM working environment needs: decisions (settled, authoritative) + missing-context-list (referenced/needed but unresolved) + conflict-log (sources contradict). Our single block conflates all three. Decision needed: adopt the data-room three-artifact frame at substrate-shape level, with uniform field tightening across all three blocks, replacing the current single-block conflation.
decisions.decision: ADOPT the data-room three-artifact frame for decision-lifecycle substrate. Concretely: (1) recognitions.json (NEW) — pre-decisions / decision-needs; status enum [recognized | drafted | reviewed | ready_for_enactment | withdrawn]; promotes into decisions.json on user enactment via enacted_from link; recognition retained for lineage; (2) decisions.json (scope-narrowed) — ratified entries only; status enum [enacted | superseded]; required enacted_from + enacted_by + enacted_at; block name no longer lies about contents; (3) substrate-conflicts.json (NEW) — first-class home for in-substrate contradictions; structured per-conflict entries (affected_ids, conflict_kind, resolution_state) replace the in-body Amendment/Widening prose-stacking pattern flagged in the shape survey. Uniform field tightening across all three blocks: writer-identity enum/pattern kind:identifier; structured commits[] field; null-state convention (empty-array required, key-absent forbidden); first-class evolution_log[] entries (date, kind, by, summary, commits[]) per feedback_substrate_blocks_not_changelogs. Options_considered preserves the existing schema's soft convention; NO rejected_reason requirement added — staleness-prone redundancy with low marginal mandate-004 value (the chosen option's rationale + consequences[] already do the work; recorded rejection reasons date to enactment-time conditions and become misleading when conditions change). FEAT-008 carries the build + 49-entry migration. The full canon-mapping showing every revision element traces to established canon (DEC-0040 single source of truth; FGAP-098 cross-reference affordance; FGAP-110 valid/current discipline; FGAP-111 raw-tier-with-decomposition shape; feedback_substrate_blocks_not_changelogs; DispatchContext attestation; data-room frame) is at analysis/2026-05-26-decisions-substrate-revision-grounding.md commit 083630e — nothing in this decision is fresh architectural invention; it brings one of our oldest blocks into compliance with what we've established about substrate-shape since.
consequences: FGAP-113 addressed: block-name no longer lies about contents (decisions.json contains only ratified entries); status enum granularity restored (recognitions carries the operationally-distinct sub-states inside its own enum)
Fresh contexts reading .project/decisions.json get authoritative canon — no mixed pending+enacted requiring field-level disambiguation; DEC-0040 single-source-of-truth canon honored at block level

In-body revision prose (Amendment/Widening/narrowed stacking observed at DEC-0040/0044/0047/0003) gets structured home in evolution_log[]; block body fields hold current-truth only per feedback_substrate_blocks_not_changelogs
Writer-identity / commits[] / null-state uniformly enforced via schema; the 20 emergent shape-clusters from the survey either become schema-impossible or get first-class field representation

Cascade required: scripts/orchestrator/* (extract-decs.ts and others), Pi tools registered in pi-context/index.ts, lens validators (lens-view.ts + roadmap-plan.ts), relations.json edges, CLAUDE.md documentation, SKILL.md regeneration. Cascade is scope of FEAT-008.
Migration of 49 existing entries by current status; lineage edges preserved; in-body revision prose extracted into evolution_log[] for affected entries

decisions.title: Adopt object-form acceptance_criteria schema for task-lifecycle substrate — {id, text, status} with AC-id addressability + per-criterion status tracking + completion-invariant shift to 'every criterion passed'; scoped under .context greenfield transition (no .project migration)
decisions.context: Current .project/schemas/tasks.schema.json declares task.acceptance_criteria as string[]. Per FGAP-114 (filed 2026-05-26): individual criteria have no canonical id (no FK addressability for depends_on); no per-criterion status field (partial completion invisible); completion invariant collapses to 'verification field present' rather than schema-level 'every criterion passed.' Surfaced immediately by TASK-080 split (2026-05-26) — TASK-082 depends on TASK-081 at task-grain only when criterion-grain would enable cheaper sequencing. Same FK-as-free-text anti-pattern documented in FGAP-040/041/046. VER schema's criteria_results[].criterion currently restates the AC text verbatim (existing duplication). Decision needed: adopt the object-form AC schema at substrate-level, scoped consistently with the broader substrate-shape revision arc (DEC-0050 / FEAT-008 reframed under .context greenfield rather than .project in-place migration).
decisions.decision: ADOPT object-form acceptance_criteria schema for task-lifecycle substrate. Concretely: acceptance_criteria becomes object array with items {id (pattern ^TASK-\d{3,}-AC-\d{2,}$), text, status (enum [pending|passed|failed|blocked])}. depends_on semantic expanded to union(task-id | ac-id); resolver handles both kinds. Completion invariant shifts to 'task.status=completed requires every acceptance_criteria[].status === passed' (AJV draft-07 cannot enforce conditionals at item-status level; encoded in schema description + downstream validator). VER coexistence: task.criterion.status = live lifecycle state (mutates as work progresses; canonical home for current truth); VER.criteria_results.status = point-in-time attestation snapshot (immutable once filed; canonical home for historical verifier attestation); criterion field in VER.criteria_results becomes AC-id FK eliminating the existing text-restating duplication. Two distinct facts with intentional vocabulary overlap — NOT duplicates. Enums intentionally differ (VER has 'skipped' which task doesn't; task has 'pending'+'blocked' which VER doesn't). AC-id padding: 2-digit canonical (^AC-\d{2,}$ within compound TASK-NNN-AC-NN id) — sub-ids exempt from DEC-0026 3-digit top-level rule (sub-ids have bounded per-parent population). Scope: .context greenfield only — .project tasks unchanged, no data migration; ships with the broader .context transition (DEC-0036 + DEC-0050/FEAT-008 umbrella) as same-arc structural-shape revision. FEAT-009 carries the build.
consequences: FGAP-114 addressed: AC-id addressability enables FK depends_on at criterion-grain; per-criterion status surfaces partial completion; completion invariant strengthens from presence-check to content-check
VER duplication eliminated: criteria_results[].criterion becomes AC-id FK rather than free-text restating; task is single source of truth for criterion text; VER references it
depends_on resolver semantic expansion: must handle both task-id and ac-id endpoints
No data migration in .project; .project task schema unchanged; existing string[] AC entries remain canonical in .project until full .context transition completes
.context greenfield schema-design absorbs the object-form AC shape from day one alongside DEC-0050's recognitions/decisions/substrate-conflicts split — same revision arc
AC-id padding canon (2-digit sub-id, exempt from DEC-0026's 3-digit top-level rule) precedent established for other future sub-id patterns in substrate

decisions.title: Adopt Hybrid 3 composite-tool design for pi-agent-dispatch — per-KIND triples (library + Pi tool + CLI script) + 5-layer forbidden-wholesale enforcement + author-tool-grant writer.kind=human gate
decisions.context: TASK-089 (commit babe385) landed pi-agent-dispatch capability composition with TOOL NAME granularity (composeToolGrant intersection + operation-vocab.ts 55 canonical Pi tool names). JI-023 user verbatim names finer granularity ('A bash command. Not bash.' + 'There can be a registry of them'). FEAT-006 north-star requires bounded-composite scoping (constrained orchestrator + privileged agent + bounded grant). User-directed design exploration 2026-05-28 produced Hybrid 3 candidate: a small set of canonical composite KINDS in framework code + config-declared instances + writer.kind=human authoring gate. Fresh-context canon-eval (analysis/2026-05-28-hybrid-3-composite-tools-canon-eval.md commit 3b4b32d) evaluated v1 against 19 canon items (A-S): 0 fatal conflicts; 9 ALIGN + 3 ALIGN-with-caveat + 7 TANGENTIAL. Two design-completeness items surfaced: (1) DEC-0019/0020 dual-surface canon requires per-KIND triples (library + Pi tool + CLI script as a unit); (2) feedback_no_parallel_ungated_paths requires explicit forbidden-wholesale enforcement so 'bash'/'write'/'edit' cannot silently degrade the design. Plus mandate-007 violation: v1 deferred substrate-canon forbidden-list 'if/when project-specific need emerges' — that is identified-need-deferred. Refined v2 design (analysis/2026-05-28-hybrid-3-refined-composite-tools-design.md) closes all three items with no canon evolution required. This decision adopts v2 as canon.
decisions.decision: ADOPT Hybrid 3 v2 composite-tool design for pi-agent-dispatch. Concretely: (1) per-KIND TRIPLE per DEC-0019/0020 dual-surface — each composite KIND lands as library function in packages/pi-agent-dispatch/src/composites/{kind}.ts + Pi tool registration at extension-load reading config.tool_operations[] entries + CLI script at scripts/orchestrator/composite-{kind}.ts taking same instance_params as CLI flags. Initial 4 KINDS: read-files, git-log, grep-paths, command-allowlist. (2) 5-layer forbidden-wholesale enforcement per feedback_no_parallel_ungated_paths defense-in-depth — L1 framework TS const FORBIDDEN_WHOLESALE_OPERATIONS=['bash','write','edit','shell','execute'] + L2 engine test invariant in operation-vocab.test.ts (TOOL_OPERATION_DEFAULTS contains zero forbidden tokens; husky pre-commit gate) + L3 runtime guard at extension load reading L1 ∪ L5 union (throws extension-load error on violation) + L4 JSDoc citation at operation-vocab.ts + L5 substrate config.tool_operations_forbidden[] (project-specific forbidden additions; AmendRegistry descriptor {kind:'string-array'}; gated by author-tool-grant Pi tool writer.kind=human). (3) NEW author-tool-grant Pi tool mirrors TASK-089 author-agent-spec pattern: wraps amendConfigEntry mutations on tool_operations[] + tool_operations_forbidden[]; refuses writer.kind != 'human' per DEC-0047; refuses ops in L1 ∪ L5 union. (4) NEW composite-loader.ts at extension-load: reads config.tool_operations[]; per entry of kind matching a registered KIND, registers Pi tool wrapping kind's library function with instance_params closure-bound; refuses to register if canonical_id is in L1 ∪ L5 union. (5) Schema extension: config.schema.json + samples/conception.json + packaged samples/schemas mirror gain tool_operations_forbidden array property; AmendRegistry union + REGISTRY_DESCRIPTORS gain tool_operations_forbidden entry. Zero canon evolution required — all elements are concrete enactments of existing canon (DEC-0019/0020 dual-surface; DEC-0040 substrate single source of truth; DEC-0047 capability authoring human-only + default-empty; DEC-0048 engine-tests-are-gates; FEAT-005 operation-granular grant from empty; JI-023 bounded composites; JI-024 empty default; feedback_no_parallel_ungated_paths defense-in-depth). FEAT-010 carries the build. Both addressed-gap FGAP-116 (operation-granular capability vocabulary gap) and addressed-feature FEAT-010 are filed alongside this decision.
consequences: FEAT-005 capability composition gains bounded-composite granularity (the form JI-023 names: 'A bash command. Not bash.') — operation-vocab.ts entries become KIND-typed instances bounded by instance_params, not just bare tool names

DEC-0019/0020 dual-surface canon honored per composite KIND: library + Pi tool + CLI script land as a unit; adding new KIND = source change + release; adding instance of existing KIND = config write via author-tool-grant (writer.kind=human gated)

FEAT-006 north-star (constrained orchestrator + privileged agent + bounded grant) becomes implementable at the bounded-composite level: TASK-091 assembly can grant agents 'read-files-from-analysis-and-docs' or 'git-log-with-these-paths-and-this-since' rather than wholesale 'read'

feedback_no_parallel_ungated_paths protected by 5-layer defense-in-depth: any addition of wholesale tokens ('bash','write','edit') to TOOL_OPERATION_DEFAULTS fails L2 engine test (husky pre-commit gate); any registered op in L1 ∪ L5 union fails L3 runtime guard at extension load; framework forbidden-list is source-change-only (release-gated, not config-mutable); project-specific forbidden additions land via writer.kind=human author-tool-grant

DEC-0047 capability authoring expanded with author-tool-grant Pi tool (analogous to TASK-089 author-agent-spec): writer.kind=human enforcement gate for config.tool_operations[] + config.tool_operations_forbidden[] mutations; sub-agents have no escalation path to widen the capability registry

Schema cascade: .project/schemas/config.schema.json + packages/pi-context/samples/conception.json + packaged samples/schemas mirror gain tool_operations_forbidden property; AmendRegistry union + REGISTRY_DESCRIPTORS gain corresponding entry — substrate path is mutable via existing amendConfigEntry surface

Net file count for first build: ~14 new + ~5 modified (4 composites + composite-loader + author-tool-grant + their tests + 4 CLI scripts + operation-vocab + extension entry + config schemas + context.ts REGISTRY_DESCRIPTORS)
